complaint_text: I am writing because my plan — Silver Home Broadband 24 — has increased in price mid-contract and I am finding the whole situation very worrying. I do not understand the technicalities and have phoned four times over the past three weeks (5 March, 12 March, 19 March and 23 March); each time I was told someone would get back to me but no one has. On 19 March I spoke with Claire who suggested that the increase was ‘standard,’ which is not an adequate explanation for changing contractual terms without clear notice. I am elderly, not confident online, and rely on clear, patient communication. Please either honour the original contract rate or allow me to leave without penalty; I would appreciate a written confirmation and simple step-by-step guidance on how to proceed if cancellation is necessary.

How considerate of you to charge a termination fee that was never mentioned when I asked about switching plans. I left your service on 10 March after being told by a sales agent that there would be no early termination penalty; instead I have been billed £240 on my final statement dated 31 March. I raised this with your team on 1 April (advisor Nina, ticket 99421) and was told someone would call back. No call has been received and the charge still stands.

If your records show a different promise than the one I received, please show me the signed agreement or the precise clause that permits a £240 fee. Otherwise I expect the fee removed and a corrected final bill issued. I would prefer not to pursue external dispute processes, so please reply with a clear resolution within seven days.

Oh good, another bill surprise. I cancelled my contract on 14 March 2026 because I am moving into university halls — a perfectly reasonable lifecycle event, I would have thought. I spoke to Sam in your retention team on 14/03 (call ref: 440-77-01) who assured me the cancellation would be processed and that no termination penalty would apply. Imagine my delight when a charge for £140 appeared on my account on 22 March, described as "early exit fee." That was unexpected. Truly.

I have used up the usual routes: web chat, the standard complaints form and two calls. Each interaction has led only to polite repetition and no action. At this point I want this escalated to someone with authority who can do what Sam said. Either cancel the fee or tell me quickly why you think it is justified. If you cannot sort this out within five working days I will be lodging a formal complaint with the Ombudsman. I would prefer not to have to do that, but I will if I must.

I am a student on a tight budget and this is my second request. My contract (account 12345678) was terminated on 12 March 2026 and a single early termination fee of £275 was taken on 13 March. I raised this once before via webchat on 18 March (case 98765) and received no substantive reply. The charge is unaffordable for me; reverse the fee and confirm cancellation in writing within 48 hours.

I am writing after repeated unsuccessful attempts to resolve what appears to be fraudulent activity on my family account. Timeline: on 28 March I received a text indicating a SIM change for one of my children’s numbers; I did not authorise this. On 29 March I noticed two outgoing high-value calls billed to an unfamiliar premium-rate service totalling £960. I reported the unauthorised SIM swap to your support line on 30 March (call ref 90512) and was told a fraud investigation would start — I was given an incident number, F-00377, and advised to contact my bank. I followed up by email on 2 April and via web chat on 5 April (chat ref 332188) because no holds had been placed and new charges continued to appear. On 7 April I spoke briefly to a specialist named Mark in fraud who said he would escalate to the fraud team manager; I have not had a substantive update since then. I have filed a police report (station: Hounslow, ref 2024-14839) and my bank has provisionally blocked the affected card, but the continuing exposure on the account presents a real risk to my family’s finances and personal data. Please take the following immediate actions: freeze any further account changes, restore any altered security settings, reverse the fraudulent charges pending your investigation, and supply a written timeline of the steps you will take and the expected completion date. If you are unable to confirm these actions within 48 hours I will need to escalate this to the ombudsman and pursue formal complaints through the police report and my bank. I am calm about the process but require urgent and decisive action to prevent further harm.

On 02 March 2026 I telephoned your sales line to sign up for a student plan and spoke with an agent named Jack. He described a 12-month plan with 40GB of data at £15 per month and reassured me twice that there would be no early termination fees and that content filters would be applied for parental control. Based on that conversation I accepted and gave my bank details. The confirmation email I received that evening, however, shows a 24-month contract at £35 per month and no filters; it also includes an early termination charge. I called sales back on 04 March and spoke to Hannah who admitted there had been a mistake and said she would escalate to complaints. On 10 March I called again, spoke to Paul in complaints, who promised a manager would call me within five working days; no manager has called and I have received only automated updates. I am a student with very limited funds and the difference between what was promised and what has been applied is unaffordable. I have exhausted the normal channels and am now requesting a manager review and correction of the contract to the terms promised by Jack on 02 March. If that is not resolved promptly I will ask for intervention by the Ombudsman. Please provide the name of the manager handling this and a firm timescale for correction.

I joined your network in 2007 and have paid my bills on time ever since. The decision to switch off 3G in my area on 08 March has rendered my handset effectively unusable for mobile data and for several of my essential apps. I have spent three weeks attempting to resolve this through the normal channels: web chat on 11 March with Rakesh, a phone call on 13 March with Priya, and an email on 17 March. Each time I was given promises of a call back or a 'provisioning ticket', none of which resulted in a substantive solution. I am now formally requesting escalation. I need either a 4G/5G-capable replacement SIM and a temporary handset loan while you arrange a compatible device, or an immediate transfer to a tariff that covers a handset upgrade at no extra cost, together with reimbursement for measurable losses since 08 March. I am exhausted by being passed between advisors and cannot rely on standard queues any longer. Please supply the name and contact details of the manager assigned to my complaint and confirm the timescale for resolution within three working days, or I will refer this matter to the Telecommunications Ombudsman.

I write as the account holder for the Family Plus plan (account ending 3214) to escalate an unresolved billing discrepancy that is causing immediate financial strain. On 12 March 2026 I contacted your billing team and spoke with Ms Patel (case ref 842173) about a duplicated line charge of £74.50 that appeared on the statement dated 05 March 2026; I was advised a correction would be applied within 5 working days but no credit has been posted and a further direct debit of £298.92 was taken on 20 March 2026. This is the second formal attempt to have the matter corrected—my earlier email of 14 March 2026 remains unanswered—and the continuing uncertainty is affecting our household budget and the children’s access to school services. Please investigate, reverse the erroneous charge, provide a corrected bill and a written confirmation of the refund within 48 hours; if you cannot meet that timescale, advise a named contact and escalation route so I can take the matter further with confidence.

Hi — I’m Tom and I’m getting fed up repeating myself. On 9 April my family plan auto-renewed for another month and I never authorised that. I called on 10 April, then again on 16 April and once more on 25 April; each time I’ve had to explain that I don’t want automatic renewals for add-ons and I explicitly asked the agent Lucy on 16 April to remove the auto-pay toggle from our account. It wasn’t. Today I log into the app and there it is, another charge pending. I manage this plan for three kids and I keep careful track of spending, so these surprise renewals throw off our budgeting. I’m annoyed, honest. I don’t need a lecture about terms and conditions; I need you to stop billing me without permission and to refund any recently taken amounts for services I never approved. Please sort this out properly and tell me what you’ve changed so I don’t have to call again next month. Account: Thomas Reed. Previous call refs: 99321, 99378, 99415.

On 15/03/2026 my number was ported away without authorisation and I subsequently discovered several unauthorised transactions totalling £350 on my account. I reported the incident immediately and spoke with the fraud desk on 15/03/2026 and again on 18/03/2026 (agents recorded as Tom R. and Sarah P.). Both times I was told an investigation had been opened but I have received no substantive update, no reversal of the charges and no confirmation that my number has been secured against further unauthorised activity. The unauthorised port removed my access to two-factor authentication for work accounts, which is a material security issue. I therefore request an urgent, documented investigation, immediate reinstatement or suspension of the number to prevent further loss, and a full refund of the fraudulent charges. I have exhausted standard support routes and now require escalation to a senior fraud manager; if I do not receive a written response outlining remedial action within five working days I will lodge a complaint with the ombudsman and consider reporting the breach to Action Fraud.

I have a long-term health condition and I rely on a predictable budget. On 20 March 2026 your system renewed my contract and took £29.99 from my account without my authorisation. I phoned on 21 March, spoke to Naomi who said she would reverse it; I called again on 24 March and spoke to Ahmed who put me on hold for 30 minutes then promised an investigation. This is the fourth call about the same payment. Please cancel the renewal immediately, refund the £29.99 to the same bank account, and confirm in writing that my plan will not be renewed automatically in future. I need confirmation in large-print or by post as I struggle with online access. I will await your response within five working days.

I am registered disabled and I am writing because I have been demonstrably penalised for staying with you. My contract renewal in January 2026 increased my monthly line rental to £46.50 even though new customers on the same plan were offered £35. I phoned on 02/03/2026 and spoke with Rachel in Retentions (call reference LP-48192). Rachel admitted I was paying more than current promotions but declined to apply the new rate, saying the change was ‘outside policy’. I then emailed customer support on 05/03/2026 and was told to ring back. I have made three separate attempts to resolve this through the normal consumer route and each time I have been passed from one script to another. I am on a fixed income and this disparity is not trivial to me. You are aware of the guidance under the Consumer Rights Act and Ofcom’s expectations about fair pricing; I expect an immediate price match to the current advertised rate from the date my renewal took effect, an explanation of why loyal customers are charged more, and compensation for the overpayment dating back to January. I have kept all call recordings and email threads. I will accept a response from a manager within 14 days. Failing a satisfactory outcome I will refer the matter to the Ombudsman.

This is my first formal complaint but I expect a precise and prompt remedy. I have been charged a so-called "activation fee" of £29.99 on my bill dated 02 March 2026, plus a "router handling" charge of £14.50 that was not disclosed at signup on 25 February 2026. The signup page did not present these amounts clearly; I clicked to accept on the assumption the only recurring cost was the advertised monthly price. Under the Consumer Contracts Regulations and the Consumer Rights Act 2015 you must provide transparent pricing before the contract is concluded. Your behaviour appears to be an unfair trading practice: an additional contractual term introduced after purchase without explicit consent. I request an itemised breakdown of all charges applied to account number 07-xx-xx, a full refund of the undisclosed fees within seven calendar days, and confirmation that no similar hidden charges will be applied in future invoices. If you refuse, explain under which contractual provision you justify these charges and cite the precise clause on my agreement page where these fees were disclosed. I will not accept evasive responses; I expect documentary evidence and a timeline for refund.

I am a small business owner and my case has been mishandled for too long. On 2 March I reported a billing error where a duplicate charge of £310 appeared on the account; I was promised a refund by 12 March by adviser Harpreet. That date passed and I called again on 15 March, was transferred three times, given two different reference numbers and told someone would call back — no call came. I escalated on 20 March and spoke to Emma who said the refund was authorised, yet my bank statement shows nothing. This complaint has been passed around with contradictory information and no resolution. I have exhausted the usual routes and now request a named manager to review the file, confirm the refund in writing, and provide a timeline for when I will see the money. If I do not receive a satisfactory reply within five working days I will submit a formal complaint to the ombudsman and include all correspondence.

I've now used your chatbot four times — 28 February, 7 March, 14 March and most recently 21 March — because I need a home visit booked that I cannot do myself. Each session dutifully instructed me to unplug and restart equipment and check plugs, which I cannot manage due to my limited mobility. The bot then closed the chat claiming the issue was resolved. Charming little loop, but it doesn't help someone who needs a human to arrange an engineer. Please escalate this to a human advisor who can schedule a visit and note the accessibility requirements; I would prefer not to repeat the same scripted exchange next week.

My name is Margaret Ellison and I am 76 years old. I do not use apps; I do not sign up for anything online without help. Yet on 12 February 2026 my landline and broadband were automatically renewed for another 24 months and I only discovered this when a paperless invoice appeared at the end of last week. I did not authorise a renewal. I have the confirmation letter from when I first took the contract in 2019 and there was no clear point at which I agreed to roll-over. I rang customer services on 15 March and spoke with Sophie in billing, who told me to try the online account settings — which I cannot navigate — and later with Daniel in Retentions on 18 March who said the auto-renewal was standard practice and could not be reversed without a fee. I asked repeatedly for a manager. I was promised a call-back on 20 March by a manager called Mr Patel; no call came. I have followed every direction your staff have given and now find myself being charged for an extra year I never consented to, and for a service I may wish to leave. I am asking now, formally, for a full reversal of the renewal and a refund of any up-front charges taken for the extra period, plus written confirmation that my account will not be auto-renewed in future. If this cannot be resolved internally I will have no choice but to contact the Telecommunications Ombudsman. I am tired of being passed from person to person and treated as if I do not understand. Please escalate this to a manager who will read the notes I have left and actually act on them.

I cancelled my contract and switched provider on 05/02/2026. Your records are continuing to debit my bank account. A direct debit was taken on 10/03/2026 despite clear notice of cancellation and confirmation emails from the new supplier. I have contacted your billing teams repeatedly: I spoke with Claire on 12/03/2026 who advised a refund; I spoke with Michael on 18/03/2026 who said the matter was resolved; neither outcome occurred.

This is a formal notice: the charges taken after termination are unlawful. Under the Consumer Rights framework and your own terms, continuation of billing post-cancellation constitutes an unlawful deprivation of funds. I demand immediate repayment of all sums debited since 06/02/2026, a written explanation of why the direct debit mandate was not cancelled, and evidence that my account has been closed. Provide the refund within seven days and supply a named manager to handle this matter.

If you do not comply I will escalate to the Ombudsman and consider a claim in the Small Claims Court and referral to Trading Standards. I have exhausted standard channels and am requesting escalation to a manager and to the Ombudsman now. Please treat this as time-critical and respond in writing within 48 hours.

I am writing to report a recurring fault with the handset provided when I switched to your service on 04/03/2026. The device (Samsung A53, IMEI ending 1123) exhibits persistent issues: the microphone intermittently cuts out during calls, the screen flickers on wake, and the battery drains from 80% to 25% within three hours of light use. I reported these faults to customer support on 05/03/2026 (call handled by Raj Patel, incident reference REF987654) and was advised to perform a factory reset. I completed the reset as instructed but the defects remain. This is my second formal contact about the handset; the previous troubleshooting has not resolved the matter. Given the symptoms and prior diagnostics, I request either a replacement device or a written specification of the next technical steps you will take, together with a timescale for resolution. Please escalate to your device support team and respond with a clear remedy within ten working days.

To whom it may concern: I have been a customer since 2011 and purchased a handset (Samsung Galaxy S21, IMEI ending 6789) from your online store on 15/01/2024. Over the last two weeks the device has developed repeated random shut-downs and the battery is bulging. The handset is within the original manufacturer warranty and under the terms of the sale it is expected to be of satisfactory quality. Under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 I therefore require a remedy: specifically, repair or replacement within a reasonable period. I have not previously reported this matter to your support — this is my first approach. Please advise how you intend to proceed, including timescales for collection/replacement and confirmation that any replacement will be of equivalent specification. I expect a response within 14 days before I consider exercising my statutory rights further.

I am the owner of a small digital marketing agency and I depend on your leased line during business hours. I raised this issue on 12 March (ticket 784512) after a recurrent drop in throughput affected client meetings; I spoke with Mr Patel who acknowledged the problem but did not provide a remediation plan. This is my second formal attempt to obtain a resolution. Please escalate this to your business continuity team, provide a full diagnostic log and a proposed corrective schedule, and confirm any service credits due under your service level agreement. Timely clarity is required so I can plan client work; I expect a response within three working days.

When I switched from ClearWave on 02/02 I expected a smooth transfer. Instead I spent the next six weeks unwinding a mess that began with a sales call on 02/02 from an advisor who gave his name as Liam. He assured me I was being put onto the ‘All-Inclusive Family’ tariff with unlimited calls and 500GB data. I signed up based on that assurance. On 12/03 I received a bill showing pay-as-you-go charges totalling £38 for calls and texts I believed to be included. I called support three times (cases 112233, 112244 and 112255) and spoke to Priya, then Marcus, then a chat agent who said a manager would call but nobody ever did. I also visited your King’s Cross shop on 18/03 and left with no resolution. This has cost me both time and money; what began as wanting to consolidate accounts now feels like being boxed into the wrong product because of a mis-sold plan. I have exhausted the standard channels and request this be escalated to a manager immediately. If we cannot agree a correction and a refund of the erroneous charges I will pursue an independent review through the ombudsman. Please confirm corrective action and an itemised credit to my account within five working days.

I have been a customer for over a decade and recently my mobile signal at home has become unreliable, with frequent call drops and very slow data since early January. I understand coverage can vary, but this is affecting simple tasks like mobile banking and arranging deliveries. This is my first time reporting it; could you investigate mast performance in the SO21 postcode and advise any planned improvements or temporary solutions such as an indoor booster? I would expect an estimated timeline given my long-standing account.

On 12 March someone performed a SIM swap on my number and on 13 March two transactions totalling £4,200 were authorised using my phone as the second factor. I reported it to your fraud team on 13 March and spoke to Jake (fraud), case 8842; Jake placed a temporary block but said fraud would follow up with full handling. I have had no substantive update. My bank holds my account but the fraud continues because the SIM change was not properly investigated. I am partially sighted and rely on this number; my savings are at immediate risk. Immediately freeze any further changes to the account, suspend outbound changes, reissue the SIM only in person with ID checks, provide the full call recording of the SIM change within 24 hours and give me the incident reference to pass to the police. If you cannot resolve this now, escalate to your senior fraud manager and explain how I lodge a complaint with the Ombudsman. Contact while this is active: 07988 222334.

I am a student living on a tight budget and I am beyond frustrated with the unexplained charges on my account. I contacted you once already on 5 March 2026 via web chat (reference REF-45217) and spoke to Aisha, who said she would 'pass this to billing' and that someone would call — nobody has called and the extra charges remain.

The issue: my February invoice shows "administration fee" £9.99, "connection fee" £3.50 and an additional "contract adjustment" £4.20. I have combed through my contract and the tariff sheet I signed on 12 September 2025 and none of these items appear. I have never agreed to a connection fee after my SIM swap in January, and the so-called adjustment does not match any usage anomaly.

I have attached (to the web form) screenshots of my contract page and the invoice. To make this painfully clear: my monthly plan is £12.00. The bill shows 12 + 9.99 + 3.50 + 4.20 = £29.69. I am a student; an unexpected extra £17.69 is not trivial. I need a full breakdown of each of these fees: why they were applied, the exact date each fee was incurred, and the legal or contractual basis for each charge. I want the incorrect charges removed and the money refunded to my debit card. If they are valid, show me the section in my contract or a screenshot of the change I allegedly accepted.

This is my second attempt. I am not prepared to tolerate vague promises that 'billing will investigate' without concrete answers. Please provide a full itemised response and a confirmation of refund or reversal within 14 days so I can plan my monthly budget accordingly.

I am a student on a limited budget and must have an answer today. I signed up for a 12‑month plan on 01 September 2025 and informed the sales adviser at the time that I might need to leave term early for a placement. When I requested to terminate the contract on 12 February 2026 because my placement will be remote and I need a cheaper provider, I was presented with a termination penalty of £240. I have called four times over the last three weeks — on 13/02 (call with Mark), 19/02 (web chat with Lila), 25/02 (phone callback from complaints), and 03/03 (again with customer services). Each contact produced the same charge and no practical solution. I do not have savings to cover this amount and must pay rent and tuition. Please either waive the termination fee, provide a reasonable repayment plan that does not jeopardise my rent, or authorise an immediate transfer of my number without penalty. I need confirmation of a decision by close of business today so I can plan finances accordingly.

I write as the proprietor of a small retail business that depends on your fixed-line and broadband services for card payments, order processing and remote bookkeeping. On 9 March 2026 I noticed a series of unauthorised changes to my account and multiple outbound premium-rate calls billed to my line totalling £3,640. I reported this on 11 March (case FRAUD-20260311) and have escalated the matter on 18 March and 23 March, speaking with your fraud team members Jordan and Ms Patel. Each time I was assured an investigation would follow; no substantive remediation has been completed and the unauthorised charges remain on my account. I have preserved call records and transaction logs and I am formally requesting that you: (a) immediately suspend all third-party call routes on account 07xxxxxxxx, (b) reverse the disputed charges in full, (c) confirm within 48 hours that you have lodged a report with Action Fraud and notified the Information Commissioner’s Office under the Data Protection Act if personal data was exposed, and (d) provide a subject access copy of all account-change logs back to 1 January 2026. Continued delay is causing significant cashflow disruption to the business and exposes me to regulatory reporting obligations. If you do not act within the requested timescale I will seek a statutory remedy through Ofcom and consider civil recovery. I expect written confirmation of the immediate mitigations and a timetable for a full refund by close of business within two working days.

To whom it may concern,

I write as a recently transferred customer (port date: 5 March 2026) who was explicitly advised during the sales conversation that any exit fees from my prior supplier would be covered up to £500 by your company. Sales reference 5MARCH-TS. I switched from GreenLine Telecom on that basis. I have now received an itemised charge of £450 purportedly billed to me for early exit from that previous contract. I have raised this through your normal support channels on 10 March and 17 March (case numbers 20260310-A and 20260317-B) and spoken to advisors Fiona Lewis and Omar Shaikh, who were unable to resolve the matter and told me it was being escalated. Despite those assurances, the £450 remains outstanding on my account and is about to be referred to debt collection, which will have serious implications for my credit file.

I have exhausted the usual routes and therefore request immediate action from a senior manager. Either (a) honour the original commitment to cover the exit cost in full, or (b) supply a written explanation, by close of business tomorrow (26 March 2026), justifying why the undertaking given at point of sale is not being honoured and confirm the mechanism by which I may dispute the charge formally with an independent adjudicator. If this is not resolved promptly, I will lodge a complaint with the Ombudsman and consider seeking legal advice regarding misleading sales representations.

Yours sincerely,
Marcus Alvarez
Account: 400-88-301

My name is Emma Cartwright and I manage our family plan (account ending 4821). I am contacting you because my son’s handset (Samsung A14, bought through your online store on 07/01/2026) has developed a fault. Since 09/03 the microphone cuts out on every call: the other party can hear him for a few seconds, then nothing, then it returns. Video calls and voice notes sometimes work, sometimes not. The phone is only 11 weeks old. I have not taken it apart or installed anything unusual. I want this handled sensibly. Please confirm whether you will send a replacement handset, offer a repair, or provide a loan handset while you assess it. I can wait a few days for a technician, but I need clear next steps and a timeline. I have two children on this contract and depend on at least one working phone for school pickups; I do not need this to drag on without communication. Please reply with the options and how to progress. Thank you, Emma Cartwright.

This is getting tedious. I was sold the 'Unlimited Home Fibre' plan over the phone on 4 February 2026 by an agent named Ben Carter (call record 04/02/2026, ref 102334). During that call I explicitly told him I wanted unlimited data at £25/month and no fixed-term exit penalties. Ben confirmed this deal and said the order was placed. Instead, my contract shows 'Business Lite' at £45/month with a 24-month minimum term — a plan I never accepted and which is unsuitable for my household. I raised this with support on 10/02 (chat ref 11001), 18/02 (phone ref 11444) and 02/03 (email to billing, ref 11987). Each time I have been given different explanations: billing system error, policy reasons, or promises that the paperwork would be amended. No correction has been made and I have been charged the higher monthly fee for March (£45.00 taken on 01/03/2026).

I am a busy professional and have already spent hours on calls and emails. To be specific, I require: a) immediate reversion to the product I was sold (Unlimited Home Fibre) at the £25/month rate; b) a full refund/credit for the excess amounts charged since activation; c) removal of any early-termination obligation arising from the incorrect contract; and d) written confirmation of these corrections and the timescale for completion. Please escalate to a senior billing manager and resolve within five working days. If you cannot fix this promptly I will request a formal investigation and pursue compensation for my time and the incorrect billing.

I run a small consultancy from home and depend on a reliable connection. Over the past three weeks the broadband has been far below the package speed, and it is affecting my calls and work scheduling. On 18 March 2026 at 10:15 I ran a speed test that returned 4.3 Mbps download and 0.8 Mbps upload against the expected 60 Mbps. I repeated the test on 22 March at 09:30 and got similar low figures. I called technical support on 19 March and spoke to Luke, who arranged for an engineer visit on 24 March. The engineer, called Mark, checked the line and reported everything as nominal but could not explain the slow throughput during my business hours. I have tried the basic troubleshooting steps you provide and followed the engineer's suggestions. I am making this complaint politely but firmly: I need either a credible plan to restore consistent speeds during weekdays or a proportional refund for the degradation. I have exhausted the standard routes and now ask to speak to a manager or for this to be escalated to the Ombudsman if a satisfactory remedy is not proposed within seven working days.

Order ID: SW-20260302-112. Timeline and current position below.

I switched my fibre service to you on 2 March 2026. The engineer visit was scheduled for 10 March and completed on that date, but since then the connection has suffered intermittent dropouts during peak hours (typically between 18:00 and 22:00) and sustained latency spikes up to 350 ms. I raised this as a fault on 12 March (ticket 209344) and spoke with Raj on 15 March who said the issue would be escalated to network operations. No further updates have been provided and the ticket shows no progress in the online portal.

To be clear: this is my second attempt to get an update. The lack of communication is causing disruptions to evening work calls and home schooling video sessions. Please supply a full timeline of actions taken to diagnose the problem, the person or team responsible for the next steps, and a realistic ETA for resolution. If an on-site visit or remedial works are required, provide available appointment slots within the next five business days. I would appreciate a written update by close of business on 29 March 2026.

I took out the Student Saver 20GB plan on 02/03/2026 because I’m trying to keep costs down while at uni. My first bill arrived on 11/03/2026 for £44.98 and includes a £9.99 “activation fee” and a mysterious £6 administration charge that were never mentioned when I signed up. I live on a student budget — between rent, food and course materials every pound matters. I phoned customer services three times: spoke to Helen on 07/03 (who said she’d look into it), then Raj on 10/03 (who told me the fees were “standard”), and again on 15/03 via the complaints portal where the reply just repeated the same boilerplate. Your online sign-up flow does not show these extras clearly and that felt deceptive. I’ve exhausted the normal channels and now want this escalated to a manager; if you cannot remove the unexpected charges I will ask the ombudsman to review the sale practices. Please respond with a detailed breakdown and refund of the unexplained fees within 14 days. I’m a student — this is not trivial to me.

I've called three times over the last two weeks because our family plan was targeted by scam texts that led to several premium-rate charges on 11 and 12 March. I manage the kids' allowances and those charges wiped out our top-up. I spoke to Liam on 13 March who removed two of the charges but said the rest needed investigation; then I called again on 16 March and was told a different ticket owner would call back — they didn't. My daughter keeps getting messages asking to 'verify' her number. I need two things: immediate refunds for the unauthorised premium charges and a block on premium-rate and international-pay services on all lines on my account while you sort this. I'm not asking for sympathy, I'm asking for the account secured and a timeline. Please treat this as urgent — I can't keep chasing while my kids lose their credit and I worry about identity theft.

I am disabled and dependent on my mobile for essential monitoring and contact with carers. For the past six months my coverage at home has deteriorated to the point where calls drop and SMS messages fail to deliver. I have been patient and methodical — I first reported this in a web chat on 1 February (case reference visible in my account), then spoke with an adviser called Priya on 10 February who logged the issue as intermittent coverage, and later with Reuben on 3 March who said an investigation would follow. After three more attempts through the app and two engineer visits arranged by your team, nothing has changed. On 14 March a scheduled remote health check failed to send data because the signal dropped; my care team could not receive the telemetry for two hours. That delay put me in a vulnerable position and caused real anxiety. I have documented timestamps, call logs and the packet loss logs Reuben requested and uploaded them to the portal. I have been told twice that there is 'no fault found', despite clear evidence of repeated outages between 17:00 and 22:00 on most days. This is not acceptable. I need a lasting technical solution — either a signal booster installed, relocation to a different cell, or a SIM/plan that uses a partner network in my area — and I need your proposed remedy within five working days. I am asking that this be escalated to a senior network manager immediately and that you include the Ombudsman contacts in your response if you cannot prioritise a fix. I cannot continue to rely on inconsistent service where my health and safety are at stake.

Hi — I need this cleared up properly. Over the last three weeks I have called and sent messages about mysterious extra charges on my bill and keep getting the runaround. First call was 04 March with Leah, who said she would remove an itemised ‘service admin fee’ of £3.50 and refund it; second call on 11 March with Daniel who told me it was a one-off and wouldn’t recur; third call via web chat on 18 March with Aisha who promised an investigation and case number (V-1803-55). The amount keeps appearing: a small sum, I accept, but I am on a low-income support package and these additions add up. I am fed up of having to explain my circumstances repeatedly to different people and being told different things each time. Can you please either send me a clear breakdown showing what each charge is for and why it was applied, or cancel any fees that were applied without my consent and process a refund for the last four weeks? I don’t need urgent action; I can wait while you sort it properly. I do need an end to the circular talking, though — give me a single point of contact or a final decision so I can stop chasing this every few days.

I appreciate that contracts exist, but charging an elderly customer an extortionate early termination fee when they are suddenly moving into full-time care is neither compassionate nor reasonable. My name is Alan Pierce and my home broadband contract began on 01/04/2025 (24 months). On 15/03/2026 my GP advised immediate residential care for my wife; we informed your customer services and asked for a compassionate termination. I called three times — 18/03/2026 (spoke with Sophie), 21/03/2026 (spoke with Ben) and again on 22/03/2026 — and each time I was told empathetic words but then quoted a fee of £360 to leave the contract. We've provided medical letters and proof of the care home placement, yet no sensible accommodation has been offered. I cannot reasonably pay that sum on top of care costs. Please escalate this to a supervisor and advise how to contact the Ombudsman — I have exhausted your frontline help and expect a waiver or a pragmatic settlement today. I am not asking for charity, only for a sensible resolution given the circumstances.

I am writing as the primary account holder for a family bundle with four lines (account reference 79-3045). Over the past three weeks I have identified a sequence of fees which were not disclosed at the point of renewal: a so-called 'admin charge' of £17.95 applied on 03 March, and a recurring 'service management' fee of £4.99 applied from 10 March. I have called customer services on 4 separate occasions (14 March, 17 March, 21 March and 23 March) and spoken with agents Rina Patel and a supervisor in billing, who logged references BIL-1423 and BIL-2147. Each time I was told that the charges were legitimate and that I would receive an itemised breakdown by email; that breakdown has not arrived.

As a parent managing multiple lines, transparent billing is essential. I require a full explanation in writing of the nature and contractual basis for these fees, a full refund of all unagreed charges applied since 1 March 2026, and confirmation that no such fees will be levied in future without express consent. Please respond within seven working days with the documentation I requested. If you cannot substantiate these charges, I expect them removed immediately and a written apology sent to my email address on file.

I am a student and on a tight budget. On 14 May 2026 I received notice that my monthly plan rate will increase from £18.00 to £24.50 from 1 July. I have reviewed my contract and the terms cited in your email do not appear to permit an increase of this magnitude mid-term without my consent.

I have raised this with customer services via webchat on 20 May and by email to billing@yourprovider.co.uk on 25 May; both avenues have been exhausted without a satisfactory explanation. Under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and the rules around unilateral contract changes, I am requesting a formal review by a manager. If you cannot justify this variation in accordance with the contract terms, I require either the rate to be reverted to £18.00 or a contract termination without penalty. If this cannot be resolved, please confirm how I can refer the matter to the Communications Ombudsman.

I run a small catering business in East London and I am contacting you because the mobile signal at my premises has been unreliable for several weeks. I can manage with the current service for now, but I would appreciate a clear plan and a technician visit window because my business depends on the connection for contactless payments and order coordination. I am quietly disappointed that it has reached this point, but I can wait for a solution if you can confirm next steps and likely timescales.

I’m a parent on the family plan and I’m contacting you again about the slow broadband at our address. Since around 4 March the connection has been much slower than normal — streaming buffers, video calls drop and the children’s online lessons stutter. I called on 10 March and spoke to Emma in technical support who logged a ticket (ref 84721) and mentioned an engineer would be arranged, but I haven’t had any further update. It’s not an emergency, but it’s disruptive to the routine; please can someone confirm the current status and an estimated time for resolution.

My name is Margaret Davies and I am 78. I try to manage my single mobile line on a fixed pension but your March bill is wrong and I don’t know what to do. I received invoice 003287 dated 12 March 2026 showing £97.45 due. My contract is for a £21.99 monthly plan plus £1.49 line rental; I normally pay £23.48. There are four extra items labelled as "premium content" on 02/03, 06/03, 08/03 and 11/03 totalling £69.97. I do not subscribe to any services. My granddaughter, Jenna, checked my phone settings on 13 March and there were apps installed that I did not put there. I cannot understand the billing portal — the fonts are small and the layout confuses me. This is the first time I have complained to you. I do not have savings to cover unexpected charges. Please do the following: (1) remove the premium items, (2) refund £69.97 to my debit card immediately, (3) put a block on my account so no further premium purchases can be made without my explicit spoken permission, and (4) send me a clear, printed statement to my home address at 17 Willow Road, Norwich NR3 4LP explaining what happened in plain language I can understand. If you need to call, please ask for my granddaughter Jenna who helps me — her mobile is 07788 332244. I am frightened about being billed again and I need this fixed straight away.

I switched my mobile to your network on 2 February because I was told coverage would match my previous provider. Imagine my surprise when my basic phone — the same device that worked before — started losing signal on 14 March after what you describe as a 3G shutdown. I have not called about this before; this is my first report. It is inconvenient to find that the advertised network change renders an otherwise functional phone unreliable, particularly when the sales representative assured me the move would be seamless. Please advise how you intend to restore usable service for legacy devices or provide a clear, no-cost upgrade path so I can use the mobile as I did prior to switching.

I am writing again because I cannot accept being charged more simply because I have been a customer for longer than new sign-ups. This is my fourth contact about the same issue: I first raised it on 12 March with Zoe on web chat, followed by a call on 19 March with Mark (reference 78-412-3), a follow-up email on 26 March that was acknowledged by Rita, and another chat on 31 March where I was told someone would escalate this. Nothing has changed.

My account (ending 4521) has been with you for three years. I pay for the 100/20 fibre plan. New customers are being offered £10 off per month on exactly the same plan; my last bill was £46.50 and there is no loyalty reward, only the regular increases you roll out. I have checked the welcome offers, the online terms and your published tariffs. I am not asking for anything unreasonable: either match the current new-customer offer or provide a clear, documented justification for why long-standing customers are effectively penalised.

I am tech-savvy and I have screenshotted the promotional pages (saved 13 April at 14:02). I will not be fobbed off with vague phrases about “reviewing offers” again. If you need time to investigate, say so and give a date when I will receive a definitive response. I expect a written resolution within 10 working days. If you cannot match the offer, give me a contract exit with no termination charge. I would prefer to stay, but only if treated fairly.

I phoned to cancel my landline because I don’t want two of the same services and I’m fed up with the confusion. The story is this: on 2 February I rang and spoke to Karen in cancellations who said she would arrange it and send a confirmation. Nothing arrived. On 8 February I rang back; a man called Paul told me it was on hold because of a technical note and that it would definitely be done by 10 February. It wasn’t. On 17 February my neighbour, who did me a favour and tried the online account for me, found the line still active and charging me. I called again on 20 February and spoke to a different advisor, who opened a complaint and gave reference 78-9083. That reference hasn’t been updated. I am old and weak with these digital hoops. I have followed every instruction, waited the promised three working days twice, and still the subscription is active. I am asking now for a manager’s intervention and, if necessary, I will take this to the Ombudsman because I have exhausted your normal channels. I do not want estrangement or argument. I want the cancellation completed, a final bill showing only what I owe, and a written confirmation sent to my home address. I am upset that a simple, reasonable request has become a month-long series of false promises.

Good afternoon. I manage the family plan on account number 78-34-662 and noticed my monthly fee has risen despite being loyal to your service since 2016. There are four lines on the plan and my current tariff is £34.99 per month; a new customer quote online for the equivalent bundle is £26.99. I would like you to review this disparity and apply any loyalty adjustments available so that my bill aligns with current offers for new customers, or explain why this is not possible for existing customers. I am raising this as a first contact about the matter and would prefer a straightforward resolution: either a tariff match or a clear explanation of alternative value you can offer. I manage this account for the family and need predictability in our monthly cost, so please respond with options within the next two billing cycles. I am a little disappointed to see the difference, but I am happy to accept a reasonable solution.

This is my first contact regarding cancellation of the family mobile bundle attached to account ending 9421. On 18 March 2026 I attempted to cancel via the online portal; the interface accepted my request but did not issue any confirmation or final date, and the dashboard still shows the service as active. As a parent organising household accounts I need a formal written confirmation of contract termination, the effective end date, and an itemised final bill showing any early-termination charges (if applicable). Please treat this as my formal notice to end the service and send the necessary documentation to the registered email address within 14 days so I can make alternative arrangements.

I am a full-time student and I write again because this ongoing handset defect has now left me effectively without a phone. I first reported the issue on 03/02/2026 (call logged with advisor Ravi, reference 547812), followed by calls on 17/02/2026 and 03/03/2026 to your technical team; I have made five attempts in total over the last seven weeks. Each time I was told the handset would be replaced or repaired under the terms of the Consumer Rights Act 2015, but you have failed to provide a remedy. The handset (Galaxy A21s supplied as part of my Student Essentials plan) powers off randomly, drops network access, and will not hold a charge for more than 30 minutes — on 15/03/2026 it cut out while I was waiting for a Vital University Zoom tutorial and again on 21/03/2026 I could not call 999 when I had a medical incident nearby. As someone on a tight budget I cannot afford to buy a replacement outright, and your delay is causing academic disruption and serious safety risk. Your online diagnostics returned an error code (FW-ERR-11) which your agent Sophie acknowledged on 03/03/2026 but promised only a software reset, not a replacement. Under the statutory right to a satisfactory quality product I ask for an immediate replacement handset or a full repair completed within seven days, plus a pro rata credit for the weeks I have been without reliable service. If you do not comply I will escalate to Trading Standards and Ofcom and consider a claim for damages in the small claims court. Please note I expect a substantive response and a named repair or replacement appointment by close of business 27/03/2026. If you require further details my original case ref is 547812; I am available on my email (mariam.hassan@studentmail.ac.uk).

I am contacting you regarding the replacement handset provided under my contract which has developed repeated faults and is causing a practical problem for me. As a vulnerable customer I request a priority replacement device or a full refund for the handset within ten working days, together with confirmation that any essential numbers for healthcare contacts will be retained and forwarded if necessary. I would appreciate a formal written update and an accessible point of contact for follow-up.

I have been a customer since 2008 (account 4532-789-001) and am requesting immediate cancellation of my broadband and mobile bundle. As of today I have had no written confirmation, no final bill, and no cancellation reference despite four contacts over three weeks. Please confirm the service end date, any final sums due, and issue a written cancellation reference within five working days. If you cannot complete the cancellation, explain the reason and provide an escalation contact so this can be closed promptly.

Dear team, I am the account holder for Family Plus 40 (account 04-1234567). On 1 March 2026 your announced 3G switch-off left my elderly mother’s 3G-only handset without service. I phoned twice — on 2 March (spoke to Ravi, case ref 221342) and again on 6 March where Samantha advised a workaround that has not materialised. This is time-critical: either provide a loan 4G/5G handset immediately or confirm an immediate contract adjustment and compensation. I have exhausted the usual routes and now require escalation to a manager or the ombudsman if you cannot resolve this within 24 hours.

So your 'HelpMate' virtual assistant thinks telling someone with limited mobility to 'try a full reset' is adequate customer care. I have multiple sclerosis and rely on your online help because lengthy phone conversations are physically difficult. I raised this via the chat on 17 March (ticket 847392) when I needed my direct debit updated; the bot offered a link to the FAQ and then closed the session. Nobody followed up. This is my second attempt at getting a sensible response. I don't expect miracles, only that a human reads the notes you clearly already have and either makes the change or trains the automated system to stop cycling through the same useless steps for vulnerable customers.

I joined your service on 12 February after transferring from my previous provider and explicitly chose a rolling monthly plan. I would appreciate a clear account of why the renewal occurred, the evidence you have that consent was given, and a prompt refund if there is no such evidence. So my bill went up mid-contract, did it. I signed an 18-month agreement on 15 September 2025 for £22 per month and on 1 March the direct debit increased to £27. I rang three times over the last fortnight — spoke to Darren on 05/03, then Pat on 11/03, and a different advisor (no name given) on 18/03 — who all kept telling me it's 'an update to terms' and couldn't explain why my fixed-price period was seemingly ignored. I’m not tech-savvy and I don’t want to be fussing with apps or account settings; I expect clear answers when I call. If the contract is being altered while I’m halfway through it, that’s not right. Please reverse the rise to the original £22 and confirm you will honour the remainder of my agreement, or tell me plainly why that cannot happen. I’m not asking for miracles, just fairness.

When my internet began to slow down I thought I was doing something wrong. I live alone and do not understand much about routers. On the evening of 03 March I sat with my granddaughter while she helped me video-call my doctor; the connection froze so often the doctor could not see me clearly and the appointment had to be cut short. The next morning I called the helpline, spoke to Alan on 04/03 and he told me to restart the router and that speeds would improve. They did not. For two weeks now the pages load piece by piece, videos stutter, and on 15/03 your engineer visited but said the line 'looks fine' and left without changing anything. I am 78 and have a prescription delivered by a pharmacy that messages me about delivery times; last week I missed the message because the broadband would not load. This is not just irritation — it affects my health appointments and my ability to keep in touch with family. I raised this before but no one has fixed it. Please arrange a proper diagnostic at a time I can manage, and do not promise a timeslot and then cancel at the last minute as happened before. I need the internet at a usable speed to access my doctor and to speak to my grandchildren. Help me get it working.

You sold me the wrong package. On 15/03/2026 your sales rep Neil recorded my order as a residential 100Mbps line when I explicitly said this is for my café and I need the business 200/200 product. I raised this on 18/03 with customer services and got ticket 992733, but nothing has been corrected. My till depends on a stable upload and the current setup drops during busy periods, costing sales. I need the order swapped to the correct business tariff and confirmation of installation dates within three working days. If you cannot fix this immediately, provide a clear compensation plan for lost trading days and move me to a temporary business-grade connection until the permanent fix is in place.

Been with you five years on Business Wave. My monthly charge went up suddenly by £8 for no reason. I’ve contacted support four times over the last three weeks (calls on 2/3, 9/3, 16/3 and 20/3) and keep getting the same unhelpful responses. This loyalty penalty is unacceptable while I run a micro-business that depends on reliable billing. Fix the bill or I’ll have to look elsewhere.

On 15 February 2026 my plan auto-renewed and I was charged £180 for a 12‑month contract I did not agree to. Please cancel the renewed contract immediately and refund the £180 to the card charged, and confirm by email the date the refund will be processed. If you cannot resolve this quickly, outline the escalation route and timescale.

I have been with you for over ten years. When the 3G shutdown was implemented I discovered my alarm panel and car tracker stopped connecting on 31 January 2026. This is my second contact about the same issue. The lack of connectivity is inconvenient and leaves equipment unusable. Please either provision a compatible SIM immediately or arrange an alternative solution and advise on any compensation for the period without service. A response within five working days would be helpful so I can plan accordingly.

My name is Liam Hart and I am a full-time student on a very tight budget. On 15 March 2026 my handset dropped from signal and since then I have had no data or calls because you have shut down 3G in my area (postcode: LS6 3QP). I only realised the impact when I could not access my student bank app to authorise a payment for rent on 16 March — the payment bounced and my landlord now says he will take further action if it is not cleared by Monday 29 March. I need you to explain why there was no personalised notice, why my area was switched off without practical alternatives offered, and what you will do immediately to restore service or provide an emergency alternative SIM or credit so I can access online banking and submit my rent payment. If you cannot provide a working solution within 48 hours I will have to involve my university welfare team and seek compensation for the financial penalties I now face. Please put this to a manager and respond with a clear, time-bound plan today. I expect acknowledgement of this message by return and a named contact who will handle the case until it is resolved.

So my first month with you included the pleasure of an automatic renewal I never agreed to. I have raised this three times — 8 March (chat), 15 March (phone, spoke with Liam), and 22 March (email) — and each time I was told a refund would follow, which has not happened. I do not believe I gave consent for an auto-renewal and I would like the charge reversed and the renewal cancelled. Please confirm the refund and cancellation in writing and state when I can expect the funds to be returned to the card on file.

I need to cancel one line on our family account and would like that process completed promptly. On 22 March I used the online cancellation form but did not receive a confirmation number, and the line remains active with a further payment due on 1 April for £27.50. As the account holder and parent, I require the line to be ended and any future billing for that line stopped; please confirm cancellation and provide a refund for the pro rata amount already charged for April. I expect written confirmation of cancellation and the date it will take effect.

My name is Dorothy Kemp and I am writing because my direct debit was taken for another year of the package I explicitly told your adviser in February I did not want to renew. I do not understand how this happens. I first rang on 2 February to ask about canceling; I spoke to Sophie and she said she would note my account. On 15 February I called again after noticing an email I did not understand; Mark told me everything was fine. On 1 March I saw a new charge of £28.99 on my bank statement and I phoned straight away — I spoke to Lucy who told me the system shows an automatic renewal and referred me to your app. I do not use the app. I am 78 and not confident with smartphones or online forms; I told every person that and yet my card was debited without my permission. I am upset and exhausted by having to explain myself repeatedly over several weeks. I want the renewal reversed and a clear note on my account that I want no further renewals. Please do not ask me to use the app; arrange the correction by phone or post. If you need to call, please call during weekday mornings when my hearing aid is switched on — I find evening automated systems impossible. Thank you.

I write as the proprietor of a small consultancy (Greenleaf Consultancy) to note a recurring issue with lengthy wait times when contacting your support line. Between 5 April and 12 April 2026 I placed four separate calls regarding billing queries and service clarifications. Each time I remained on hold for in excess of 25 minutes before being connected; on two occasions the call was disconnected while still in the queue. These delays are not critical to operations but are inefficient and take time away from client work. I have raised the matter through your standard complaints channels and spoken with frontline staff on 7 April and 13 April who logged the issue, yet the situation has not improved. I request a formal review of call handling metrics for my account, an explanation of what has caused the prolonged wait times, and escalation to a manager if you cannot provide a reasonable remedial plan within 14 days. I would welcome written confirmation of the action you will take.

So you cancelled my line on 2 March 2026, which is helpful, because I expressly asked for the family plan to be pared back. Your website even sent me a cancellation confirmation reference: CF-83912. Imagine my surprise to see the direct debit taken on 25 March for £42.50 for the very line I asked to remove. I appreciate systems can be slow; I also appreciate that charging for a service that was confirmed as cancelled is not best practice. Please reverse the payment and confirm the refund date, and while you are at it update the account so the rest of the family plan is charged correctly. I am a parent juggling school runs and work; handling billing errors offers me no pleasure, only inconvenience. I expect a correction on the next statement and a brief note explaining what went wrong.

I am contacting you about a mid-contract price increase that has been applied to my Student Unlimited 24 plan without my informed consent. My contract started on 5 September 2024 at a rate of £15.00 per month and was explicitly represented to me as a fixed amount for 24 months when I signed up. On my latest bill dated 1 March 2026 the monthly charge rose to £19.00, an increase of £4 per month which equates to an extra £48 annually — a significant amount on my limited student income.

This is my first formal complaint. I have limited finances: I work 10 hours a week in retail alongside my studies and budget tightly for rent, bills and course costs. A mid-contract uplift was neither communicated to me in a clear, timely manner nor agreed. I require a detailed explanation of the contractual clause that purportedly allows this change, a copy of the notice you sent (date and method), and immediate reinstatement of the original £15 rate or an option to exit the contract without penalty. If you cannot honour the original pricing, please provide a compensatory arrangement that recognises my financial position, such as a reduced rate, account credit for the excess charges since 1 March 2026, or a goodwill gesture sufficient to cover the shortfall for the remainder of the contract.

Please respond with a substantive solution within five working days. I would prefer to resolve this directly, but I will escalate to the Citizens Advice consumer service and Ofcom if necessary.

I am contacting you for the first time about a critical failure that endangers my safety. I am registered as a vulnerable customer under the Equality Act and rely on my mobile to connect to carers and the telecare service. On 21/03/2026 I discovered that outgoing calls to my nominated emergency contact and the telecare number fail to connect; the handset shows ‘Network Registered’ but calls do not complete and SMS delivery reports are not received. There has been no prior contact with your team because I only became aware of the issue when an expected call from my carer did not come through at 18:00. I require priority handling: a same‑day engineer visit or a temporary loan handset and an immediate check of my account provisioning. Your duty of care to vulnerable customers and the regulatory guidance around prioritising people in such circumstances mean this cannot wait. Please treat this as an urgent safety escalation and confirm within two hours how you will restore my ability to contact emergency and care services. If you are unable to provide an immediate remedy, advise what interim safeguards you will put in place so my carers can reach me.

I transferred my line to you on 2 March after being told the agent, Daniel Morris, had set me up on a fixed-rate business plan for 24 months. On 9 March I checked my online account and the tariff shown was a month-to-month consumer plan with higher call charges. I rang on 10 March and spoke to Hannah in sales; she accepted this was not what Daniel had agreed and opened ticket C-11758, promising rectification. No change has been made. This is a clear case of misrepresentation at point of sale and a material breach of what I was promised. I request immediate correction to the originally confirmed 24-month fixed business tariff, a refund for the differential charged since 1 March, and written confirmation that my contract terms will be honoured. If you fail to correct this within five working days I will pursue remedies through Ofcom and Trading Standards. I want a manager to contact me directly; stop passing this between teams.

Invoice dated 22/02 shows an unexplained £15 ‘service charge’. I have contacted billing twice — 24/02 and 02/03 — spoken to two agents, been given no justification, and received no refund. My business depends on predictable costs. Reverse the charge and confirm you have removed it from all future statements, or escalate to management and provide contact details for the ombudsman. I do not have time for further delays.

I am the account holder for the family plan (account ending 4321). On 12 March 2026 my eldest child’s mobile number was ported away without my authorisation and three premium-rate items totalling £246.50 have been billed on the statement dated 13 March. I have neither given permission for a port nor shared the account security phrase. This is the first contact I am making in relation to this fraud.

You must immediately suspend all activity on the affected number, block any further port requests, reverse the disputed charges and provide written confirmation of each action taken by close of business today. I also require copies of the porting logs and the recordings of any authentication calls made on 12 March. If you cannot effect an immediate freeze, escalate this to your fraud team and assign a named contact who will speak to me within two hours.

My son uses that number to contact me about school, medical appointments and childcare arrangements. The unauthorised port is a direct safety risk and has already caused financial loss. If this is not resolved urgently I will report the matter to the police and the Information Commissioner's Office and pursue all regulatory remedies available.

I reported a faulty handset on 5 March after my replacement phone started randomly losing network and dropping calls mid-transaction. I spoke to Hannah on your support line who logged the fault and promised a courier replacement within 48 hours. It is now 22 March and nothing has arrived, the phone continues to cut out every day and I am losing business opportunities. I have made one previous attempt to resolve this and was given the runaround; I am not prepared to accept another vague promise. I need either a working handset dispatched by recorded delivery today, or a credit for the days I have been without reliable service. I run a small trade business and rely on a functioning phone to take orders. Please stop delaying and do what was agreed with Hannah on 5 March.

I am formally complaining about the inability of your online chatbot to resolve a simple account query. Between 02 April and 07 April 2026 I used the web chat on four separate occasions to request a correction to my account name (it was entered incorrectly when my daughter was added). Each interaction was handled by the virtual assistant labelled 'Cora', which repeatedly suggested clearing my browser cache, checking my connection and then disconnected or routed me back to the same circular options. On 04 April I was offered a 'live agent' after 25 minutes of automated replies, only to be returned to the bot after being told a human would call; no call came. I have mobility issues and typing is slow for me; the chatbot's insistence on forms I cannot complete easily has been unhelpful and exhausting. I have tried the phone line and received scripted responses that promise escalation but deliver nothing. I have therefore exhausted the usual automated and human routes without resolution. I need a named human contact to correct the account name and a written assurance that your accessibility testing for automated systems will be reviewed. If you cannot address this, pass the complaint to your complaints manager and prepare to involve the Ombudsman. Please reply with a direct email address for a member of staff who will take ownership.

Your chatbot failed. On 23 March at 09:12 I tried to use AssistBot to swap my business number to a new SIM. The bot asked the same question three times and then closed the chat with a canned link that didn’t apply. I depend on that number for bookings. I cannot run through layers of nonsense to get a real person. Fix the bot script and tell me when a human will call. This is the first time I’ve raised it. I expect a prompt, sensible response.

I am 78 and have been a customer since 2010. I am writing because the switch-off of your 3G network is preventing me from using my emergency pendant and my mobile phone in my area — a steady loss of service that began on 10/02/2026. I have called your support line six times, most recently on 08/03/2026 when I spoke with Hannah in the technical team who suggested buying a new 4G handset; I told her I cannot manage that without clear in-person help. I have explained repeatedly that my pendant, which I use daily, is 3G only and is the reason I kept this particular SIM plan. I do not understand the technology and each automated menu leaves me more anxious. I have exhausted your standard channels and I now request escalation to a manager and a clear written explanation of what support you will provide to ensure my pendant is replaced or that I am offered a suitable alternative at no extra cost. If you cannot provide immediate assistance I will take this matter to Ombudsman Services: Communications. Please do not put me through another scripted diagnostic flow. I need a named manager and a direct contact number to arrange a simple, practical resolution within five working days.

Oh good, another barrier from your end when I simply want to leave. I submitted a cancellation request through the dashboard on 18 March and received the kind of automated reply that says everything will be processed 'shortly.' Shortly, it seems, is your way of saying 'we will ignore you.' I rang your helpdesk on 20 March and spoke with Hannah, who read from a script and assured me someone would email me within 48 hours. No email arrived. So here I am, using this second attempt to tell you: stop pretending cancellation is optional. I have no interest in protracted retention calls. I signed up for a month-to-month plan and I expect you to terminate it as requested. If your systems require a form, point to it. If a human signature is necessary, send the link. I am not paying an extra month's fee because your processes are deliberately obscure. Fix this properly this time.

When I took out the contract in June 2020 I was told the monthly line rental would not change until June 2024. On 4 March I received a letter saying my monthly fee would go up by £3.50 from April. I have not been left without a phone; this is not an emergency. But the principle matters. Changing the cost mid-contract without a clear, statutory explanation — and then failing to follow up when I asked for help — is disrespectful to a person who relies on stable bills and on the company to keep its word. I can wait a while for a proper answer, but I need the increase overturned or a clear legal justification shown to me in writing, with the cancellation rights explained. If this was an administrative oversight, correct it; if it is supposed to be lawful, show me where in my contract you permitted this. Send the reply by post and email; I am not comfortable navigating your automated system.

I have been a customer with your company for over ten years and this is my first time raising a billing issue. On 2 March 2026 my SIM-only plan was automatically renewed for another 12 months and a charge of £132.00 appeared on my card the same day. Please provide a full explanation of the contractual basis for the renewal and reverse the charge if you cannot demonstrate clear, affirmative consent in accordance with Consumer Contracts Regulations. I would like the contract cancelled and a refund of the £132.00 within 14 days, or a clear outline of how you intend to comply with the relevant consumer protection requirements. A prompt written acknowledgment of this request would be appreciated.

My retail business depends on the internet connection supplied under contract number MXS-4817, signed 15/11/2023. As a small business, I note industry practice obliges suppliers to mitigate losses rather than simply levy full remaining contract charges; please confirm what mitigation steps you have taken to reassign or resell the line. I am prepared to pay a reasonable reconciliation if properly justified, but until I receive the requested documentation I must dispute the charge. Please provide the full breakdown and legal justification within five business days. If the company cannot substantiate the fee I will seek resolution through the Ombudsman and, if necessary, pursue recovery through the small claims process. This is my second attempt to obtain an answer; continued silence is not acceptable given the financial pressure on the business.

Account 77833420: cancel the service immediately. I have called on 03/03, 10/03, 18/03 and 22/03 and each time I am told cancellation is 'being processed' but the service remains active. I need written confirmation of cancellation and a final bill showing no further charges within five working days. Do not reconnect; process the closure now and confirm by email.

I am attempting to cancel my broadband and mobile bundle because I can no longer afford the monthly payments as a student living on a tight budget. I am an elderly customer and this is the first time I have ever had to complain. I did not click it but shortly afterwards a gentleman phoned from a number appearing as 020 7946 00XX and asked for my security code, saying he was from your technical team. This is the first time I have raised this with you; please freeze any changes to my account immediately, block any SIM port requests, issue a replacement SIM if necessary and advise me step‑by‑step on how we will secure my account. I need someone patient and clear to talk me through next steps and to confirm by phone that my service is safe. Please treat this as urgent.

Hi team — quick one. My subscription to the CareAssist plan renewed on 1 March 2026 for £60 and I did not agree to that. I rang on 4 March and spoke to Liam, then on 11 March I used web chat with Jess, and again on 18 March — each time I said I wanted to cancel and get the renewal reversed. So far no refund and no written confirmation. I’m not wanting to make a fuss, I just need the charge cancelled and a clear note on my account that it won’t auto-renew again. Please email me confirmation when this is done.

I am a student and my account has been targeted by what appears to be fraudulent activity; this is urgent as the amounts involved are significant for me. On 5 February 2026 I noticed three outgoing premium-rate transactions totalling £245 on my bill. I phoned customer services on 6 February and spoke to Aisha (call ref 66211) who froze the line pending investigation; on 8 February I followed up with Mark in fraud and was told the matter was being reviewed but no refund or formal confirmation was issued. I have exhausted the routine channels and now request this be escalated to a manager immediately and that you open a formal complaint to be reviewed by the ombudsman if you cannot provide a full refund and a written fraud report within 48 hours. Please confirm what steps you will take to secure my account and reimburse the unauthorised charges.

How thoughtful of you to charge me twice for a direct debit I cannot afford. My name is Diane Holt. I have a hearing impairment and limited mobility. I called on 15 March and spoke to Lucy who assured me the overpayment would be refunded and my plan corrected. Your systems bled another £132 from my account on 21 March. I am on a fixed income. That extra withdrawal has left me without funds for my care worker this week.

You have one working day to reverse the payment and confirm in writing that my plan will be set back to the agreed amount. If you need my bank details again, take them from the mandate you hold. Do not ask me to run through the same script with any more junior advisors. If this is not fixed by 24 March I will contact Citizens Advice, my MP and consider a formal complaint to Ofcom. I expect a named manager to telephone me directly. I will not be passed around.

I have been a customer since June 2017 and have just discovered that my monthly fee is significantly higher than the promotional rate now being offered to new customers for the identical package. I am surprised and disappointed. To be clear: I am not asking for an urgent immediate fix to preserve service, only for a fair adjustment. I request a review of my account, a written explanation of the price differential, and an adjustment to match current new-customer rates or a reasonable alternative discount. I am raising this as a formal complaint for your records. Please respond with the contractual basis for the current charges and your proposed remedy. I will await your considered reply.

I have been a customer for more than ten years. Since 1 March my home broadband has been unusable during core working hours. Please arrange an engineer visit within three working days, provide a clear report of the fault and proposed remedy, and confirm any service credits I am entitled to while this persists. If there is no tangible plan of action within five working days I will refer the case to the ombudsman.

Bought a handset through your site on 20 March (order reference 9X2-3347) and the device is misbehaving straight away. I raised the early termination fee issue once already on 12 March with a representative named Ben, and since apparently the terms of my contract are more accommodating to sudden life changes than the customer service notes suggest, here I am again. I am asking, for the second time, that you waive this fee on compassionate and practical grounds. Ten years with you. Service stopped working after the 3G switch-off on 20 March. My personal alarm device lost its connection. I cannot reach my monitoring centre. I am a student on a tight budget and have been shuffled between teams for six weeks with no meaningful outcome. This is an urgent formal complaint. My landline and broadband failed completely on 20 March 2026 and remained down for more than 36 hours. I expect a senior manager to call me today and details of the compensation you will offer for the service failure that jeopardised my safety. Account number 455-781-992: I am disputing recurring overcharges that have been billed since 1 February.
