Please confirm by telephone (I prefer a landline call) or by post. I would appreciate a clear timetable rather than further verbal assurances. Yours sincerely, Agnes Ward

I am writing as I do not understand why my bill for March 2026 shows an extra charge of £31.99. I would appreciate a phone call to confirm between 10:00 and 12:00 if that is possible.

Hi — I’m ringing the alarm bell again because my son’s handset (Samsung A32, IMEI 356789012345678) has been useless for two days and your support haven’t fixed it. I reported this on 15 March to Aisha on your support line (case REF 884312) and was told a replacement would be posted within 48 hours. It never arrived. Having no working handset on one of the lines is putting our childcare arrangements at immediate risk. I’ve been juggling work and kids all morning trying to sort this, which is not acceptable. Please arrange an immediate same-day replacement or temporary loan handset and confirm collection of the faulty device — I can’t be left unable to reach school or emergency contacts. If you need anything from me the fastest way is SMS to my main line (07700 900123).

Ten years with you and apparently I’ve developed an odd hobby: listening to your hold music. Hello — I’m a student and I’ve got no broadband at my flat since 03 March, so I’m writing about it for the first time. I am contacting you as the account holder for a family plan (three lines) to report a faulty handset and to request a prompt remedy. Thank you for attending to this matter; I would prefer a clear schedule for repair rather than open-ended delays.

My daughter's Samsung A12 stopped taking a charge on 08/03/2026. I reported it on 11/03 (ref 912345) and was told a replacement would be sent; nothing arrived. Account holder: Amina Patel, family plan on 07xxxxxxx. I'm frustrated this is still unresolved — I need a concrete dispatch date for a working handset.

I switched to your network from ClearSignal on 12 February 2026 and was sold the Unlimited Stream plan by an on-boarding agent called Daniel. I have been a customer of yours for over a decade (account opened 02/03/2012). I have been forced to rely on neighbours and a battery-powered landline connection, which is not a long-term solution given my health needs. I have exhausted ordinary complaint routes and now require escalation to a senior manager and a formal referral to the Communications Ombudsman if this is not resolved within 24 hours. I demand either immediate provision of an alternative handset/SIM that restores voice and emergency-call capability, or an immediate pro-rata refund of my contract charges plus a written explanation of why I was not given adequate notice and support. If you do not respond with an actionable solution today, I will commence a formal complaint to the Ombudsman and seek compensation through the small claims track for additional costs and distress.

My name is Owen Patel, account holder. On 4 March you informed me that my monthly service fee would increase by £3.50 from 1 April — mid-way through a 24-month contract that began on 1 June last year. I have spoken to customer services three times since then (live chat on 5 March with Nina; phone call on 8 March with call handler Mark; email response from general support on 10 March). I have no interest in being transferred between teams. If you cannot produce the contractual basis for this increase within seven working days, escalate this to a manager and confirm in writing that you will either roll back the increase or offer an acceptable alternative. If this matter is not resolved satisfactorily I will refer it to the Energy and Utilities Ombudsman. I expect a substantive reply that addresses the contract terms and my specific demands, not a standard scripted response.

Cancelled my line on 14/03/2026 but your bill still charged £27.99 on 28/03. I called once on 30/03 and spoke to Tom who said it would be sorted but I have had nothing back. I am elderly and this is confusing and stressful. Refund the £27.99 and confirm cancellation immediately. A simple clear reply, please.

I run a small postal stationery business and your agent sold me a package on 10 February that was plainly misrepresented. I raised this on 2 March (case 33219) and received a generic troubleshooting email with no substantive remedy. You are in breach of contract and potentially in breach of the Consumer Rights Act 2015 for supplying a service not as described. I require the following within 48 hours: (1) provisioning of the agreed 200Mbps service or an immediate rescission of the contract with full refund of all sums paid; (2) compensation for demonstrable lost revenue of £2,750 and documented costs of mitigation; (3) a written explanation identifying how Daniel Harper was authorised to make the claims he made and assurance of staff retraining. If you do not meet these demands I will commence proceedings through the small claims court and notify Ofcom and Trading Standards. This is my second formal attempt to resolve and I expect substantive action now.

I have been a customer since 2010 (account 00457219) and am escalating this matter because your frontline teams have failed to resolve a complaint after multiple attempts. The original issue — repeated incorrect billing and failure to apply the loyalty discount promised when I renewed in January 2026 — was raised with Tom Harris on 2026-02-10 and again with Priya on 2026-02-18. Since then I have had three further callbacks where I was given inconsistent information, promised a manager callback that never arrived, and provided with different compensation figures. I am no longer willing to be passed between teams. As a long-standing customer I expect your complaints procedure to be handled competently and within the published timeframes. I now require escalation to a senior complaints manager and a written response setting out: (a) why the loyalty discount was not applied; (b) a corrected account statement; and (c) the compensation you will pay for the service and handling failures. If I do not receive a satisfactory response within seven days I will refer this matter to the Ombudsman and consider terminating my services and seeking redress for breach of contract.

Switched from another provider on 1 March. No updates. Contacted complaints line twice: 8 March (Liam) and 18 March (Priya). Both said ‘engineer will call’ and nothing. I would prefer to remain with you, but only at the previously agreed rate. Please either reinstate the original monthly charge or allow me to terminate the contract without an early termination fee. I expect a response within seven days so I can plan my finances accordingly.

Hello — I'm Mrs Joan Fletcher and I'm ringing about the handset you sent me (Model: A12, IMEI 352938102938475). I joined on 3 February on your 'Fibre 80' tariff and have repeatedly measured downstream speeds of 8–12 Mbps during off-peak and peak times (tests on 9 March and 18 March using speedtest.net). I expect an acknowledgement within 48 hours and a substantive response within 10 working days.

I have been a customer for over fourteen years and I am fed up with my handset continually failing. I have tried your basic troubleshooting repeatedly; the problem is hardware-related and has not been fixed despite multiple contacts over the last three weeks. Please resolve this without further delay.

Hi — I’ve been with you for 12 years and I’m on a fixed income because of a mobility impairment. My renewal offer arrived on 8 March 2026 quoting £40 per month, yet I can see new-customer deals advertised at around £28 per month. I don’t want to move providers, but this discrepancy feels like a loyalty penalty and is difficult on my budget. Could you review my account and either match the best available new-customer rate or explain any legitimate reasons for the higher quote? I have been trying to get a human being to help me for weeks. I am registered as having chronic respiratory illness and rely on my home line and your broadband to run the oxygen monitoring app and to stay in touch with carers. On 8 February I reported intermittent dropouts; on 15 February I followed up because the problem was worse; on 22 February I used the online chat; and on 3 March I contacted you again after a full day without a usable connection — four contacts in total. Each time the so‑called assistant handled my messages. It refuses to understand that I cannot perform the suggested fixes. The bot repeatedly asks me to reboot equipment I cannot lift and to remove batteries that my carer manages. I am contacting you again because the safety alarm linked to my landline stopped working after the broadband upgrade you carried out on 14 March. I spoke to Lydia in customer care on 17 March who assured me an engineer would attend on 22 March between 09:00 and 13:00; nobody arrived and I received no follow-up call. Please escalate this immediately to a manager and arrange an engineer visit within 24 hours. If you cannot meet that timeframe, tell me who I should call now so my alarm is restored. I am asking for a written confirmation of the appointment time and the name of the engineer assigned. This is my second attempt to get this fixed and my situation is time-critical.

I’m writing because long waits to speak to anyone are no longer acceptable. On 16 March I spent 52 minutes on hold and was told by Tom that someone in complaints would ring me; again, no call. I’m exhausted by the process and feel dismissed each time I try to resolve what should be a simple matter. Because standard contact methods have been ineffective I am asking for this to be escalated to a manager and, if necessary, referred to the Ombudsman. I need a manager-level review of my account and an explanation of why promises of call-backs and responses have not been met. I can cope with a few days’ wait, but not repeated broken commitments.

To whom it may concern,

I am the proprietor of Elm & Co. Florists (account BB-43219) and am writing about repeated incorrect charges on our business account for March 2026. Our contract is Business Connect 100, which was agreed on 01/02/2026. This is the fourth time I have raised this matter: telephone contacts were logged on 02/03 (John in billing), 11/03 (email from accounts), 16/03 (telephone with Maria, who promised a correction), and 19/03 (call reference 78-19-03). Each time I have been given different explanations and no credit. As a small business that depends on your lines for customer orders, these unpredictable charges are directly affecting cashflow. I need a clear audit trail showing why these charges were applied, confirmation that the £165.00 will be refunded immediately to our business account, and assurance that future invoices will reflect the agreed tariff.

I require a formal written response to this email within five working days with the refund reference and corrected invoice. If the error is not resolved within this timeframe I will have to consider escalating to external regulators and seek compensation for any bank fees incurred as a direct consequence. Please contact me by email (accounts@elmco.co.uk) rather than by telephone during trading hours.

Regards,
R. Patel, Owner, Elm & Co. Florists

Account name: Gavin Sinclair. Account number ending 2345. Ten years as a customer, and several weeks of billing errors later, I am writing to insist on a proper resolution.

Since 02 March I have been charged recurring late payment fees and interest that are incorrect. I have contacted you repeatedly: on 05 March I spoke with Amira (reference 1122), on 12 March I spoke with Kyle, and on 19 March with Nadia. This is not an emotional outburst; it is a formal request under the Consumer Rights framework. Provide a full itemised billing history for the period January–March 2026, remove any improper charges, refund all amounts wrongly applied, and provide compensation for the repeated inconvenience. If you cannot comply, explain precisely why, cite the relevant contractual term, and provide your proposed remedy. I expect a written reply within 14 days. If you treat this as a low-priority administrative matter again, I will have no choice but to contact the relevant ombudsman — I would prefer not to, but I will if necessary.

I have been a customer of your company for over ten years and write concerning a sale made in error by one of your agents. On 9 March I spoke with an advisor named Sarah who processed an upgrade; I did not request the additional insurance product or the premium voicemail service that were added to my account, yet my bill now shows an extra £14.99 per month and a one-off set-up fee of £59.99. I raised the matter on 16 March by telephone and was told the team would investigate, but I have not received a substantive response. This is my second formal notification and I am requesting a rectification: remove the unwanted services, refund all related charges back to 9 March, and restore my original tariff of £28.50 per month. I expect confirmation of these actions within 14 days and reserve my rights under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 to pursue further remedies if the matter is not resolved. Please respond with full details of the proposed remedy and a timeline for completion.

I moved over from another network on 01/03/2026 expecting at least the same level of service. Instead I received a handset that refuses to hold a charge for more than a couple of hours, switches itself off mid-call and refuses to connect to 4G consistently. Lovely start. Battery drain began on day two; speaker began crackling on 04/03/2026. I have no intention at this stage of paying for repairs given the phone is effectively unusable. This is my first contact about the matter. I would appreciate either an immediate replacement device or clear instructions on how to return this handset for a full refund — not a runaround about diagnostics I have already completed. I will wait three working days for a substantive reply before exploring other options, including returning to my previous provider who, to my recollection, did not sell me a handset that dies at 20 percent battery.

I am writing again because my account is being charged more than new customer offers despite my loyalty. I raised this on 18/02/2026 with your retention team (case REF 20260218-LP) and was told by Rachael that the account would be reviewed and I would receive a revised bill. That promised follow-up did not arrive. I am a software developer and keep track of market pricing; similar contracts with comparable speeds and inclusive allowances are advertised at £28–£30 per month. My latest bill dated 01/03/2026 shows £42.50. I expect precise justification for the premium and a correction where no contractual basis exists for the surcharge. I request a written breakdown of the charges applied since January 2026, immediate application of any loyalty discount available to long-standing customers and a credit for the difference back to the date I first queried the issue. If you cannot produce an acceptable resolution within seven days I will cancel and transfer my services to a competitor and pursue reimbursement for any overcharged sums. Please escalate this matter internally and provide a named contact who will take ownership of the case.

I run a small landscaping business and we have lost our primary business number and online billing access due to what appears to be fraudulent activity on your systems. On 11 February 2026 an unauthorised port-out occurred; my trading line was moved without my consent and invoices started appearing against a new account. I called on 12 February (ticket 93172) and spoke to Ellen in fraud who said she would freeze the account; that freeze was not actioned. I have called three further times — 19 February with Raj (case 97410), 03 March with Fatima (case 98421) and most recently 18 March with a supervisor, Ben, who promised a forensic audit and refund timetable. I am still awaiting both. The port-out has cost me multiple lost jobs this season and I have had to explain to clients why I cannot receive calls. Financially I cannot absorb these losses, and there is clear evidence of account compromise. I require an immediate full freeze of any outgoing changes, immediate restoration of my business number, a forensic report of the breach, and provisional reimbursement for demonstrable lost earnings within seven days. I will report this to Action Fraud and the Information Commissioner's Office if you do not secure my account and provide full remediation without further delay.

I didn’t expect to have to explain this twice, but here we are. I’m registered disabled and I rely on predictable billing because managing appointments and medication is already a juggling act; I noticed an automatic renewal on 15/03/2026 for a subscription I did not agree to — the “Entertainment Pack” at £8.99 — and it drained my account. I called on 16/03/2026 and spoke with Aisha, who said she’d cancel and refund it; nothing has been refunded and the service is active even though I never opted in. The first time I raised it I was told it was a technical glitch; I accepted that explanation but the lack of follow-through is upsetting. I’m asking now, calmly but firmly: cancel the subscription, refund the £8.99 to the card ending 4321, and make sure my account is marked as not to auto-enrol into any bundles. I can wait for a proper resolution, but please don’t make me chase this again — I have limited energy and need this sorted without another long phone call.

I write as someone with a long-term disability who has tried to resolve this repeatedly and who needs a clear legal position. On 02/03/2026 I phoned to explain that I had to move into supported accommodation and asked to terminate my 24‑month broadband contract (reference 731-589-004). Since then I have called on 09/03/2026, 16/03/2026 and again on 22/03/2026; each time I spoke with a different advisor (Paul M., Zara, and most recently Daniel) who said my request would be escalated. Despite that, you are now demanding an early termination fee of £275. This charge is both disproportionate and, in my view, in breach of fairness obligations under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 when applied to a customer who cannot reasonably continue the service due to significant health needs.

I made reasonable efforts to provide evidence of my change in circumstances (hospital discharge letter, care housing confirmation supplied 10/03/2026). Your records should show these attachments. I require the fee to be waived and a written confirmation of account closure and zero balance issued within seven calendar days. If you intend to maintain the fee please provide the contractual basis for that exact calculation, copies of the original signed agreement showing my electronic acceptance, and a full itemised invoice. I am prepared to escalate to Ofcom and seek advice from Citizens Advice if you persist with an unjust charge, but I would prefer an amicable resolution given my situation. Please do not refer me to standard scripts — I need a named supervisor to handle this matter and a clear deadline in writing.

I am writing on behalf of myself, Margaret Collins, aged 79, to demand immediate correction of a wrongful contract placed on my account. On 5 March 2026 I phoned sales after being advised by your leaflet that I could transfer to a simpler, low-cost plan suitable for someone on a fixed pension. The agent who handled my call, Simon Patel (sales ID 1123), instead signed me up to a 24‑month Unlimited Data Pro tariff costing 39.99 per month and enrolled me in an insurance add‑on I never requested. I do not use smartphones, I am unfamiliar with apps and I specifically asked for the Pensioner Essentials 1GB plan that your website advertises for elderly customers. Since the sale I have rung four times — notes below — because I am on a strict income and the extra charge will leave me unable to meet other bills.

Contact history: I telephoned on 7 March and spoke to Aisha in Retentions who said she would review the sale; on 12 March I rang back and was given reference 876421 by Tom in Sales; on 19 March I called again and spoke with Rohan in Complaints who promised to escalate; on 22 March I received an automated callback that never materialised. Each time I have explained that the contract was sold in error and that I lack the digital ability to use the features of Unlimited Data Pro. No one has offered an immediate cancellation or refund.

This situation is time‑critical. I am on a fixed pension and a pending direct debit of 39.99 will be taken on 27 March, which will cause me financial distress. I require the following actions within 48 hours: immediate reversal of the Unlimited Data Pro sale, cancellation of the 24‑month contract without penalty, full refund of any payments already taken, and written confirmation that my account has been returned to Pensioner Essentials 1GB. If you cannot resolve this within the timeframe I will be forced to involve the Ombudsman and Citizens Advice for vulnerable consumers. Please contact me by telephone as I have limited internet access, and mark this complaint as urgent.

I cancelled my Essential Home Broadband 48 contract on 2 March 2026 with confirmation reference CHB-0291 and explicit instruction to stop all payments. Despite that, your billing department took a direct debit of £89.00 on 12 March 2026 and I have now had two automated debt collection reminders sent to my address. I have contacted customer services four times between 3–20 March (calls on 3 March with Connor H., 8 March with Lila S., a webchat on 14 March and an email on 20 March) and each time I was assured the charge would be reversed; it has not been. I am medically vulnerable and on a fixed benefit income — this unauthorised deduction and the threat of a collections notice is causing immediate, demonstrable harm. I require an immediate refund to the original account, an assurance that you will withdraw any collections activity, and written confirmation that my credit file will not be affected. Please escalate to billing management and provide a timescale for resolution within 24 hours.

I have called multiple times and been left waiting. On 08/03/2026 I waited 40 minutes, on 12/03/2026 another 35, and today (18/03/2026) I put the phone down after 50 minutes on hold. I am 78, I do not understand these menus, and this is not acceptable. Each time I am trying to sort why my landline shows as connected but there is a hiss on outgoing calls. I need someone to ring me back at a time I can answer: weekday mornings between 10 and 12. Please stop making me hold for so long. — Janet Reeves.

I switched to you on 3 April and have been charged an early termination fee of £295 for a contract I ended before switching. I did not authorise any charge. I expect this reversed by close of business today and confirmation sent to my email. If not, I will block the payment through my bank and lodge a formal complaint with the regulator.

I am making a formal second request for immediate waiver of the early termination charge applied to my account (A/C 842179). I first raised this matter on 4 March 2026 with Mr Patel in your retention team and was given no substantive response — simply a promise someone would call back. I have not received that call. I am registered disabled with a mobility impairment and attend weekly hospital treatment. My clinician advised that my move to sheltered accommodation was essential and unavoidable; therefore terminating the contract early was a medical necessity. The invoice dated 9 March 2026 for an early termination fee of £275 is both unexpected and financially damaging. I provided medical evidence on 11 March to your vulnerabilities team (case reference VUL-1123) and was told it would be considered. To date there has been no decision communicated. This is a sensible request: remove the fee in light of documented health reasons or explain in writing, with legal basis, why you will not. I require a written resolution within seven days, otherwise I will have no option but to seek independent advice and escalate to the ombudsman. Please escalate this to a manager and confirm receipt of the medical documentation immediately.

Hello — I need this resolved promptly but politely. On 2 April I bought a replacement handset for my daughter via your online store (order 0048721). The screen started flickering on 11 April. I rang on 12 April and spoke with Hannah in repairs who logged a fault (ref 99234) and said a replacement would be sent; nothing arrived. I called again on 19 April and spoke to Raj who escalated to tech support and promised collection and a loan phone; again, no follow-up. I am managing three lines on a family plan and cannot have one device unreliable for a child. I expect either a working replacement sent within seven days or a full refund for that handset purchase. Please confirm who will handle this and when. I do not want to be bounced between levels of support again.

I am elderly and not good with phones. Scammers have taken money from my account via your service.

I called on 03 March and spoke to Mark who said he would stop the payments. Nothing has stopped. On 12 March extra charges appeared and my bank had to freeze the card. I need the line blocked and a refund. I need someone to call me back on my landline, not text. If you do not sort this now I will have to go to the bank and the police. Please call Mrs. Edith Harper on 0117 555 2424 before the end of today.

Account name: Mark Reeves, customer since 2006. On 12 March at 07:48 my number was moved to a new SIM without my authorisation. The next thing I knew my banking app was locked and I received text alerts about failed logins to several financial sites. I have a police crime reference (CRN 2026-32058) and the bank has temporarily frozen my accounts, but I have already suffered an unauthorised transfer of £1,200 out of one account and several pending payments may bounce. This is catastrophic. I need you to: 1) Immediately suspend any changes to my account, block SIM activations and reinstate the original SIM if possible; 2) Provide a full audit trail showing all account access, change approvals, the IP addresses used and the agent IDs involved from 10 March onwards; 3) Confirm what identity checks were used to authorise the swap; and 4) Give me a direct contact in your fraud investigations team who will work with my bank and the police. I have been with you for twenty years; that loyalty means nothing if my personal data and my finances are at risk. Treat this as an emergency and escalate now. If you cannot resolve this today I will be seeking immediate legal remedies and notifying the regulator.

Hello — I run Harper & Sons floristry on King Street and our broadband dropped out on the morning of 23/03. This is the first time I'm contacting you about it. If a technician cannot attend the same day, I need a loaned router or a clear, immediate workaround so we can at least take payments. This is urgent; our livelihood depends on getting trade back on the tills today.

Dear Customer Services, I am writing on behalf of GreenLeaf Consultancy Ltd (account 78-44291) to dispute an incorrect charge on invoice 2026-03-15 for £342.50, issued 15 March. I do not wish to litigate; I require the fee waived and the transfer to the suitable provider completed without further delay. Normal complaint channels have been exhausted. Please transfer this to a manager for immediate review and confirm in writing by close of business tomorrow, otherwise I will take the matter to the ombudsman. I am disappointed it has come to that, but I need the financial hold removed and written confirmation of the waiver today.

When I signed up for the streaming add‑on in December I was under the impression the first month was a trial. I am extremely concerned and increasingly frustrated. I need a breakdown of how you arrived at £1,230, immediate suspension of any further recovery action while you investigate, and a compassionate review with a manager able to authorise a reduction or payment plan. If you cannot resolve this swiftly I will escalate the matter formally and seek third-party intervention. Please confirm receipt and outline corrective steps by close of business today.

I am an elderly customer and I require clarity and resolution regarding an early termination charge of £420 applied to my account after I moved into sheltered accommodation. I initially telephoned your advice line on 5 February and explained that I was leaving my flat on medical grounds; the advisor, Sarah Thompson, noted my circumstances and said there would be a discretionary waiver (call reference 55211). Please provide a full breakdown of how the £420 was calculated, rescind any inappropriate charges, refund the £150 already paid if the charge is invalid, and confirm a single point of contact who will complete the review. If you cannot resolve this within 14 days I will seek assistance from Citizens Advice and Ofcom. I am asking for a fair outcome and a clearer process for vulnerable customers.

I note that the 3G network in my area was retired on 15 February 2026. My medical alarm runs on a 3G-only device and lost all connectivity the same day. I accept that network evolution is necessary, however removing service without providing a compatible alternative has left me unable to summon help from my alarm device, which is a clear safety concern. Please escalate this immediately to a manager and advise what you will do to restore a reliable emergency link or provide a loaned compatible device while you implement a permanent fix. I would prefer an internal resolution, but if you cannot act now I will request an Ombudsman review for the risk this presents.

My business runs on dependable internet. I do not run it on promises. On 5 March 2026 my monthly line rental jumped from £48 to £57 with no prior discussion and no lawful notice. I first rang your billing team on 9 March and spoke to Hannah (ref: BIL-2331), who told me it was an ‘industry-wide adjustment’ and would appear on my next invoice. I called again on 16 March and spoke with Michael (ref: BIL-2389) who admitted the increase was mid-contract but said there was nothing he could do. I called yet again on 22 March and spoke to Priya, who opened complaint 004721 and promised a manager callback that never happened.

I have been ringing over three separate weeks. Each contact produced a different, vague explanation and no concrete remedy. I am a small café owner; our margins are narrow and predictable costs matter. A sudden near-20% rise to a core service in the middle of a fixed term is not a minor clerical glitch. It is a significant commercial decision made without my consent.

I want the increase reversed to the contracted rate immediately and a clear statement explaining why the change was applied. If you cannot rescind the charge, provide a formal justification, the contractual clause relied upon, and compensation for the extra amounts taken since 5 March. If you refuse either step, I will treat that as a request to terminate the agreement without penalty. I have documented every call and will escalate if necessary, but I would prefer this resolved without further delay. Please respond with a firm proposal within five working days.

As a small business that depends on your VOIP line for client calls, I am escalating after exhausting the usual support routes. I have logged three separate tickets (refs 4129 on 02/03, 4137 on 09/03 and 4160 on 18/03) and spoken to advisors named Claire and Marcus, who were polite but unable to provide a lasting solution. The line drops intermittently during peak hours, costing us missed bookings and at least two canceled appointments this month. I am requesting immediate escalation to a senior engineer and a written plan for fault diagnosis within 48 hours, plus an explanation of how you will assess compensation for measurable business losses. If this cannot be resolved promptly at branch level, please forward the file to your complaints manager or the ombudsman for review.

I switched from Beacon Mobile on 4 February and was sold your "SIM-Only Lite" tariff (account XZ-98421) precisely because I was told coverage would be comparable. I have phoned the technical team on 12 March (spoke to Sophie), 20 March (spoke to Mark) and again on 2 April — each time I was promised a solution and a follow-up that never arrived. I am politely asking for this to be escalated and for an interim remedy within 24 hours: either a replacement SIM with a guaranteed 4G/VoLTE profile, a temporary handset that works on 4G, or a pro-rata refund for days without service. This is time-critical — if this continues I cannot manage appointments and medication pick-ups; the lack of a working mobile is unacceptable. Please confirm what you will do and when, and provide the reference number for escalation.

I am following up on a complaint raised on 21 March (ticket reference C-212089) about persistent outages on our business broadband and office VoIP line. My company, Oak & Iron Ltd, is a small distributor and these services are lifelines; on 19–22 March we missed three supplier deadlines, one court-mandated delivery window, and have received several contractual penalty notices. I spoke to your agent Rebecca Miles on 21/03 who promised investigation and a timeline but we received nothing substantive and the line dropped again on 24/03. This is my second attempt to get a proper remedy. The outage appears to be on your infrastructure and is not intermittent; there is a complete loss of service for several hours at a stretch. We are accruing verified financial losses and client claims. I require the following within 24 hours: a direct escalation to your business continuity team, a clear date and time for full restoration, interim measures (temporary dedicated connection or a mobile failover solution) so our customers can reach us, and written confirmation of your proposed commercial redress for losses incurred to date. If you cannot resolve this promptly I will file a complaint with the Telecommunications Ombudsman and seek compensation for tangible business losses plus any additional costs from sourcing alternative connectivity.

When I opened my flower stall three years ago I took a business mobile line with your company because it was presented as reliable and suitable for point-of-sale connectivity. On 10 March 2026 a delivery driver arrived and there was no way to contact him because my business number had no outgoing or incoming service; the payment terminal was also offline. The decision to switch was a last resort to keep the shop operating. I am therefore requesting that you withdraw the termination fee and issue a corrected final statement reflecting service failure. I appreciate the routine nature of contract administration, but I am disappointed that a small business had to escalate to another provider before the fee was reviewed.

Please confirm within two business days that the charge has been cancelled and send written confirmation for my records.

I operate a small delivery business that depends on continuous internet and line access for order processing and phone orders. I gave notice to terminate the business line on 5 February 2026 and was informed on the sales call that there would be no early termination penalty if termination was for a like-for-like provider switch. This is inconsistent with the verbal assurance provided and with the contractual information I was shown during my call. I raised the matter on 14 March, 19 March and again on 23 March and spoke with advisors named "Maria" and "Derek" who logged my concerns but did not substantiate the fee. I request that you rescind the early termination charge, provide written confirmation of the calculation method used, and restore any interrupted service until this dispute is settled. Please respond with a substantive resolution within seven working days; if you do not, I will pursue alternative dispute resolution and consider court action to protect my business interests.

I switched to your service on 4 February and was told my previous contract’s early termination fee would be assessed by my former provider, not charged to my new account. Despite this, my latest statement includes a £160 early termination charge that I never agreed to with you. I have already spent two hours on chat and spoken to Jamie in billing on 15 March who said they would escalate to a manager — no callback received. I am asking, politely but firmly, for a full reversal of this fee and an explanation of why your setup process permitted this to appear on my account. I do not want a drawn-out investigation; I have exhausted the usual channels and expect a manager-level review within five working days. If that does not happen I will take this to the ombudsman for resolution.

My small business has been without broadband and landline since 03 March 2026. I reported the outage at 09:20 on 03 March (case number 881233) and escalated to supervisor Priya Singh at 14:00 after an hour with no progress. Since then I have experienced total loss of service for 22 days. That outage has prevented us from taking orders, processing card payments and fulfilling contractual delivery commitments. To date I have suffered an estimated £6,400 in lost revenue and have been threatened with penalties from a client for missed SLA obligations. Repeated calls have produced only automated updates and a promise of engineer attendance that never occurred. I require immediate restoration of service, a detailed root‑cause report within 48 hours, and interim reimbursement for verified losses to date. If the service cannot be restored within 24 hours I will instruct my solicitor to seek injunctive relief and will notify Ofcom and the small business ombudsman of contractual breaches and regulatory non‑compliance.

Hi there,

Look, I’ve been chasing this since early March and it’s getting tiresome. I’m on the Unlimited Fibre 200 plan and my last bill (dated 1 April 2026) jumped from £28 to £34. Each time I’ve been told someone will get back within 72 hours and every time I’ve been left waiting.

I understand businesses adjust prices, but increasing the monthly charge mid-contract without proper notice or an opt-out is poor practice. I’m technically proficient, I’ve read the terms, and I know my rights. Fix this by either rolling back my tariff to the original £28 or offering a reasonable alternative discount for the remainder of the contract. If I don’t have confirmation of what you intend to do within five working days I’ll start looking at other suppliers; I don’t want to move, but I’m not willing to pay more for no extra service.

I am a student (Ethan Morris) on a limited budget and I am contacting you for the first time regarding a repair request which has had no progress updates. My broadband experienced repeated drops starting 15 March 2026 and I reported the fault to your tech team the same day. I was given a fault reference but no completion date, and since then I have received no further communication — no appointment window, no engineer confirmation and no interim advice on how to maintain access for my online seminars. The intermittent connectivity is having a clear financial impact as I have had to buy mobile data top-ups to attend lectures. Please advise the current status of the fault, provide a realistic timescale for repair, and confirm whether a temporary data allowance or credit can be applied while the issue is outstanding. A clear update within 48 hours would be helpful so I can plan study and finances.

Hi — I run a small café and we've been completely offline since 17 March; the phone and internet went dead and my card machine, ordering system and Wi‑Fi for customers are all useless. I've phoned your helpline three times and used live chat twice: Claire put me on a ‘ticket’ on 18 March, Ben told me today that an engineer had been scheduled but then the slot was cancelled without explanation. This is costing me roughly £800 a day in lost takings and I cannot keep telling customers to go elsewhere. I've exhausted the normal routes and now need a manager to pick this up with immediate priority or I will contact the ombudsman and seek compensation for urgent business losses. Please escalate this to someone who can dispatch an engineer today and give me a firm restoration time.

To whom it may concern,

I operate a small dental practice, Woodford Dental Care (registered account number 77-209), and depend on your business broadband and VoIP services to manage appointments and take card payments. On 16 March 2026 I was notified via SMS of an engineer visit scheduled for 18 March between 09:00 and 13:00 (reference ENG-34921). The engineer did not attend and no call was received to explain the no-show. This left us unable to process online bookings and card transactions that day; we estimate lost revenue and cancelled appointments totalling approximately £420. We were assured via your automated fault portal on 18 March that a technician would return the following week, but nothing has happened.

This letter constitutes my first formal complaint regarding these unfulfilled commitments. As a business, we require a prompt, reliable fix. Please provide:

1) A clear explanation for the missed appointment and failure to notify us; 2) A guaranteed engineer date and a four-hour arrival window within the next five working days; 3) Confirmation of priority escalation to your business support team; 4) Details of compensation for business interruption — I consider £150 reasonable for the disturbance caused so far.

I am available weekdays 08:00–17:00 on the office number 0113 555 0102. If you cannot resolve this within five working days I will have to consider alternative suppliers and escalate to the business services complaints team.

Yours faithfully,
Dr Amir Patel
Woodford Dental Care

Dear Sir or Madam, I am writing because I have been charged an extra fee that I do not understand and I need this sorted. I phoned on 2 February, 16 February, 2 March and again on 12 March and spoke each time to different people — first to Helen, then to Tom, then to a lady at the Gloucester branch and most recently to Seth on 12 March. On my bill dated 1 March there is a "service administration fee" of £11.75 which I was not told about when I renewed my contract last November. I am on a fixed pension and this unexpected amount is upsetting. I cannot work out how to remove it online and the staff keep saying it is normal. I want a refund for that charge and a written explanation of why it was applied. Please correct my next bill and send confirmation in the post to Mrs Margaret Ellis, 14 High Street, BN1 1AB. If this is not resolved within seven days I will have to ask for further help from Age Concern to assist me in understanding my options.

On 2 March and again on 10 March I raised the matter with your sales team after being enrolled into an add-on service I did not request; the account was modified to include premium device insurance and a contract extension without my explicit consent. I have now called four times (notes taken with agents Daniel and Priya) and lodged two formal complaints (refs: C-20260311-A and C-20260317-B) but the charges remain. Under the Consumer Rights Act and the Consumer Contracts Regulations, a contract variation made without clear, informed consent is not binding. I therefore require the unauthorised charges removed, the add-on cancelled, and a full refund of £129.60 within seven calendar days. If you cannot comply, please provide a written justification citing the specific consent record you rely upon and a copy of the recorded call in which I allegedly accepted these changes. If I do not receive a satisfactory response by 1 April 2026 I will refer the matter to Citizens Advice and Ofcom and pursue recovery through the small claims route. Please confirm receipt and outline the immediate remedial steps you will take.

Hi — family broadband again. The speed has been hopeless for weeks and the kids' remote lessons are falling apart. I called on 9 March and was put through to Jake who ran a speed test and promised an engineer 'within 72 hours'. That engineer never arrived. I rang back on 15 March, spoke to Lisa, did the router resets, then you sent another message saying the service was 'stable' when it clearly isn't. At peak times we get less than 3 Mbps, downloads sit at 0.5 Mbps and Netflix buffers three times in a ten-minute show. I work from home two mornings a week and my wife needs the connection for calls; the children need it for class. We have three devices streaming at once sometimes — I bought the higher-tier package so this shouldn't happen. I ran Ookla tests at 08:20 and 20:45 on 20 March and have screenshots if you want them. I don't want technical jargon. I want an engineer visit within 48 hours, a temporary credit for the weeks we were unable to use the service properly, and a clear timeline of what you will do. If you cannot fix this promptly I will move providers and expect a refund for the months we were billed but couldn't use the service.

I am a disabled customer (mobility impairment) and I am writing to raise a formal concern about persistent low broadband throughput at my address. On 20 March 2026 I conducted three speed tests using the OFCOM-recommended methodology: average download 2.1 Mbps, upload 0.4 Mbps, latency 120 ms. My contract (Home Essential Fibre, clause 4.2) advertises a minimum expected download speed of 15 Mbps at my property. This is a first notification: I have not previously reported the matter but I expect you to investigate promptly and to comply with your obligations under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and your own Service Charter. As someone who occasionally needs video consultations and online prescriptions, reliable connectivity is important; while this is not an emergency, it is causing repeated inconvenience. Please confirm receipt, provide a diagnostic timeline, and state what remedial steps you will take, including whether a speed uplift or alternative provision will be offered. I would prefer written confirmation of any appointment windows and clear accessibility support if an engineer visit is required.

Second contact. First was 18 March (ref 445601) — I spoke to Tom. App still shows “in progress” with no timestamps. No update since. I want a concrete ETA or close the ticket. I’m on a tight schedule and I don’t need hand-holding. Check logs, tell me the next action and when it will happen. I can wait a few days, but don’t feed me vague statuses.

I switched my account to you from my previous provider on 02 February 2026 because the sales rep assured me coverage would be identical. I was not told that your planned 3G decommissioning would render my parents’ care alarm useless. On 14 March the alarm stopped connecting to your network. Their unit — Lifeline model L-201, IMEI 352987001234567 — is 3G-only. My mother is housebound and depends on that device for emergency calls; yesterday (23 March) she pressed the pendant and the control unit could not connect. I rang customer services immediately and spoke to Helen at 09:12 (case 20260323-44). Helen said an engineer would call but never did. I called again at 14:30 and spoke to Tom who told me 3G services were switched off across parts of the network and that interoperability with legacy alarms was not guaranteed. That answer is not acceptable. This is a life-safety issue. The fact that no contingency was offered, no temporary SIM solution, and no priority engineer appointment was made is a fundamental failure of duty of care. I have already escalated through your standard channels multiple times (calls on 18/03, 20/03, 23/03) and have documentation to show this. I require an immediate interim remedy: supply and activate a temporary device or a fallback SIM that will restore alarm connectivity within 12 hours, and a visit from a specialist engineer within 24 hours to make any necessary hardware changes. I also require written confirmation of the technical plan to maintain alarm connectivity for as long as the device is in use, and escalation to a manager if you cannot meet these timeframes. If you do not resolve this without delay I will involve medical services to record the risk, notify the Ombudsman and Ofcom, and instruct my solicitor to seek urgent injunctive relief to compel you to restore a usable connection for a vulnerable adult.

Account: 2468-1357. I am a student on a limited budget and I need clarity about my renewal terms. My contract runs until 30 June 2026. When I checked renewal options on 8 March I was offered a renewal price of £34 per month, while new customers are being offered a promotional price of £19 per month for the same package. I request either a price match to the current new-customer promotion or a one-off credit reflecting the difference from 1 April to 30 June 2026 (approximately £45), and a written explanation of why loyalty customers are not offered comparable rates. If this cannot be resolved promptly, please escalate to a manager or advise how to proceed to the Ombudsman.

I'm writing because your handling of my ongoing complaint has been unsatisfactory and slow. Our household account (family plan, four lines) has been affected by intermittent data outages since early February; I first reported the problem on 03/02 and since then I've had at least five different advisors, three different reference numbers and two promised call-backs which never materialised. The latest supervisor, Tom, told me on 12/03 that the issue would be escalated to engineering with reference 91023 and that someone would contact me within five working days. It's now 14 days later and no update has been received. Each time I manage to get through I have to repeat the same history; this is wasting my time and our children miss school work when the connection fails. I am asking for one clear point of contact, a timeline for the repair works, and a goodwill credit for the weeks we have been disadvantaged. I expect a substantive reply within three business days with a named manager and an engineer visit date; if I do not receive that I will take the matter further with consumer services.

My business telephone line is my lifeline. Over the past three weeks I have been left on hold so often and for such long stretches that it is beginning to affect bookings. On 02/03/2026 I waited 48 minutes to speak to an advisor, on 09/03/2026 it was 1 hour and 12 minutes, and yesterday, 18/03/2026, I was still on hold when the call dropped after 55 minutes. I have followed the usual routes — online chat, your automated callback queue and the standard complaints number — and have now exhausted those channels. I would prefer not to run the business by carrier pigeon, so please transfer this to a manager or advise how I can escalate to the ombudsman; a timely and practical fix would be appreciated, as this is not sustainable for a company with three full-time staff dependent on incoming calls.

Oh good, another billing surprise — exactly what I needed before rent day. I’m a student and on the Basic Student 5GB plan (£9.50/month) yet my bank statement shows a direct debit of £172.34 on 18/03/2026. That’s not a rounding error. I haven’t upgraded, I haven’t bought any extras, and I definitely didn’t authorise a three‑figure charge. First time I raise this, so I assume this is just an honest mistake on your side — please reverse the charge immediately and confirm in writing that you have refunded £162.84 (the excess) so I can pay my bills. If this isn’t sorted today I will have no choice but to dispute the payment with my bank and alert my university bursar. I expect a call back from someone who can actually process refunds, not a scripted advisor reading from a screen.

I phoned your support line on 11 March and waited 42 minutes before the call dropped, then tried again on 18 March and spent 65 minutes on hold only to be disconnected twice. I raised the issue previously (contact reference 33445566) but there has been no progress. Please provide a callback option or priority access for customers with medical needs, and confirm when I can expect to speak to an adviser without an extended wait.

Hi — putting this straightforwardly because I don't have time for long-winded customer service loops. My home broadband dropped completely on 24 March at 08:10 and I couldn't get online for work. This is my first time complaining but it's already urgent. Please escalate to the technical duty manager, send an engineer who can fix the line today, or provide a reliable replacement with static IP so I can work remotely. If you need proof of lost earnings I will provide it, but I need action first. Call me now and tell me exactly who is being sent and when.

I have repeatedly reported poor indoor coverage at my home address and would like to remind you of your contractual obligation to provide a reasonably continuous service. Please respond with the precise steps you will take, the legal basis for them and the timescale for implementation.

My name is Sofia Bennett. I am contacting you because an agent sold me the wrong tariff and activated a 24-month contract that I never agreed to. I called sales on 10-Mar-2026 and spoke with Jordan. Please escalate this to a supervisor and contact me by post or by telephone at a time we agree — I cannot manage long automated menus.

I run a small catering business and rely on your line for card payments and online orders. I run a small consultancy and rely on clear billing and support records. I am writing to request immediate assistance cancelling my home phone and broadband account. Phone hardware faulty. Bought handset 14 Feb 2026, Pixel 7A from your store. Since 21 March 2026 it locks randomly and battery dies within an hour. Cannot access work VPN or 2FA. Give me a firm timescale now.

Hi, I’m a student at Newcastle Uni and I’m fed up. I have been a customer for 12 years and I expect clearer communication. Please treat this as medium-priority — the intermittent dropouts are manageable for now but I need assurance that work is progressing within the next five working days.

My router was supposed to be fixed on 15 March after the engineer noted intermittent service, but the problem remains. I have not previously reported this, so please arrange an engineer visit within the next three working days and confirm a specific appointment window. I have been a customer of yours for over a decade and I am now writing to formally dispute repeated incorrect charges on my account. My contract — Unlimited Fibre Plus — has been continuous since 04/10/2013. On invoices dated 01/11/2025, 01/12/2025 and 01/01/2026 I have been billed an additional £27.50 per month labelled 'service adjustment' and a single charge of £199.00 on 01/12/2025 described as 'equipment replacement'. Neither the corrected invoices nor any refund has been issued. I therefore require the immediate reversal of the three £27.50 adjustments and the full £199.00 equipment charge, together with a written statement correcting my account history and confirmation that no late fees will be applied while this dispute remains unresolved. If you do not resolve this account within 14 days I will escalate the matter to a senior manager and make a formal complaint to the Telecommunications Ombudsman. My name is Evelyn Harper (customer number 04-779-122). I am on the Unlimited Fibre 200 plan which I signed up to on 12/01/2024. I have exhausted the standard channels and do not feel my circumstances have been considered. Please escalate this to a senior manager immediately or provide clear instructions on how to take the matter to the ombudsman; an unexpected mid-contract uplift like this is unacceptable for someone in my position and I need written confirmation of what you intend to do next and the rationale for changing my monthly price while still mid-contract.

I moved my account from another provider on 5 March 2026 and spoke to a sales adviser called Sam on 8 March. I would also like a clear note of any compensation for the downtime and for any reasonable costs I incur while this is resolved. This is disappointing given I only switched last month, but I am seeking a practical fix now.

I have been persistent and clear: the handset supplied under your repair promise is defective and this is putting my wellbeing at risk. I bought the device (Order 554213) on 17/01/2026; it began failing in February. I reported problems on 02/03/2026 (ref RMA 2207), 18/03/2026 (spoke to Liam in Repairs), and most recently on 29/03/2026 when Hannah in Complaints said a replacement would be expedited. After four separate messages and hours on hold, I'm almost impressed by how little information you can fit into a single case file. Family account: 'Family Connect 30GB' (account number ending 1198). Please provide a clear, dated progress note and a realistic completion date within three working days, and actually send the update rather than relying on hopeful silence.

Hi — I’ve had enough of being bounced around. My landline has been cutting out intermittently since 01 March 2026 and that’s dangerous for me because I have a health condition and need to be able to call my carers. If you cannot do that I will ask my advocate to help me take the complaint to the Ombudsman and I will seek support from my local health services to record the impact. Please call me back to confirm the next steps.

I have been a customer for more than a decade and I am raising this as my second formal approach after an unresolved attempt via your automated service. For the past six weeks my broadband has been unstable with frequent drops during evenings; I raised the issue on 2 March via the chatbot and received an automated diagnostics summary that suggested an in-home fault, but the bot would not schedule an engineer. I phoned on 9 March and spoke to Riya who opened fault report FR-23841 and assured me someone would call to arrange a visit; no call was made and the fault persisted. Please arrange an on-site engineer appointment within five working days, confirm the date in writing, and provide a single point of contact for this fault. If you cannot meet that timescale, explain what interim measures you will put in place and the expected completion date.

I am elderly and not confident with technical instructions. My broadband download is only 3 Mbps, measured on 15 March 2026, yet my plan is advertised at up to 35 Mbps. I reported this once before on 3 March (reference 77421) and was told someone would call back; that did not happen. I need a technician visit arranged or simple clear steps I can follow, ideally within the next few days.

On 4 March I contacted customer services after discovering an automatic renewal on my account for a new contract term that I did not agree to. I have a medical condition that affects my mobility and cognitive processing; unexpected charges cause significant distress and difficulty in managing my finances. This is my second attempt to have this corrected. I require confirmation that the renewal is cancelled, a full refund of the unauthorised amount, and written confirmation of the steps you will take to ensure this cannot happen again. I have rung three separate times over the last three weeks — on 02 March I spoke to Anu who told me to do a factory reset (which I did and it did not help), on 10 March Mark said he would arrange a replacement handset and gave me a reference number but nothing arrived, and on 18 March I phoned again and the man I spoke to said they'd put a high-priority note on my file. I'm not very good with gadgets and I find the instructions confusing; the repeated fixes that rely on me fiddling with settings aren't working. I need either a simple swap for a known-working phone or a technician to visit and set it up. Please phone me to arrange an appointment rather than sending more messages I will struggle to follow.

My business relies on your mobile data for point-of-sale terminals at weekend markets. On 15 March your notice about 3G shutdown appeared; I did not realise the effect until breakfast on 18 March when my payment terminal failed to connect. This is my first contact about the problem, and it is urgent. I run three market stalls and between 18 and 21 March I lost takings because card payments could not be processed. The engineer at the market suggested my current SIM appears to require a legacy network and that a replacement 4G SIM or an APN update is necessary. I have not previously reported this. I need either a replacement SIM sent next-day or a remote configuration change that allows my devices to connect on 4G/5G. The business cannot absorb further lost turnover. Please advise within 48 hours what you will do and confirm when the replacement will be dispatched. If no viable fix is provided within five working days I will have to seek compensation for lost earnings and consider alternative providers who support small businesses.

Account: 10+ years. Line down since 11 March. Phoned 12/3, online chat 14/3, complaints 18/3. Given empty promises. No resolution. I have exhausted normal channels and now request immediate manager-level intervention or I will proceed straight to the Ombudsman. Arrange an engineer within 24 hours and send written confirmation of appointment and a contact name. If you cannot meet this demand, state that clearly so I can take the next steps.

My name is Joan Harris and I am 78. I am not good with technology and I rely on you to do what you say you will. On 14 March a technician called Paul visited my home and said he would return on 18 March to fit a new telephone socket. He never came back. I called on 19 March and spoke with Sophie who promised a callback the same day; that callback did not occur.

I am asking this politely but I must be clear: I need the socket fitted. I cannot reach my grandchildren easily and I am anxious about being without a reliable landline. Please arrange a firm appointment with a named engineer within five working days and confirm by telephone at the number on my account. If you cannot meet that timescale, tell me who will take responsibility and what alternative you will provide in the interim. I expect confirmation and a date without further delay.

Called four times between 2 March and 18 March. Typical waits: 22, 27, 19 and 31 minutes. Each call either dropped or resulted in a callback promise that never arrived. This is getting inefficient. Provide a reasonable callback window or an automated booking for a support slot. Account number 764210938.

Second time I'm telling you the signal at my student flat (12 Elm Road, Bristol BS7) is terrible. I raised this on 10 March with Liam via live chat and was told an engineer would check the mast — nothing has happened. On my Student Saver PAYG SIM I miss calls and texts; I can't rely on it for basic stuff. I can't afford to change providers, so either fix the coverage or tell me when you'll actually do it.

I am 78 and not very good with smartphones or apps, so I rely on being able to speak to a person when something goes wrong. On 21 March 2026 I rang your helpline at about 09:30 because the billing section of the app would not load and I needed to check a direct debit. I was on hold for 42 minutes, then the line cut out just as a recorded message said my call would be answered shortly. I understand queues happen, but leaving an elderly customer to listen to music and vanish is unacceptable. I want to know why the queue was so long, why calls are allowed to drop without a callback option, and what you will do so I do not have to go through this again. A callback at a time I can manage, preferably before 11am, would be helpful; please don't put me through the automated loop again. I want a clear explanation and a plan to make phone support accessible for people like me.

I am writing because your automated support system has repeatedly failed to diagnose and resolve a billing error that is affecting my business. I first engaged with the chatbot on 5 March to query an extra £360 on the March invoice; the bot closed the session without creating a ticket. On 9 March I tried again; the bot provided a resolution timeline that contradicted the account notes and directed me to pay by 11 March to avoid service interruption.
