If that cannot be provided, please escalate this to the Ombudsman. I expect a substantive reply within 14 days.

I have been a customer for over ten years and need your help with an apparent scam affecting my account. I would prefer to see this closed quickly given my long history with you; please respond within seven calendar days with a clear plan of action.

This is my second attempt to obtain a proper answer about the chatbot failing to submit a complaint and the subsequent lack of follow-up. I raised the matter on 14 March (support ticket 20260314-55) and received only an automated response. Under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and relevant Ofcom guidance, I expect businesses to provide clear records of complaints and to respond within a reasonable timeframe. On 16 March I tried the automated assistant again; it acknowledged the issue but then dropped the session and produced no reference number. If you require additional information I will supply call and chat timestamps, but please do not return another generic bot message. I am aware of my rights and will seek further recourse if necessary, though I am hopeful you will address this promptly and properly.

My account is under Aisha Khan, student ID 49231; I am writing about repeated excessive call-waiting times when attempting to resolve a small billing discrepancy. I am a full-time student living at 44 Granary Lane, Manchester M1. My account number ends 6789. If there is a temporary fault or a throttling policy in place, please confirm it in writing and advise what compensatory credit will be applied to my next bill. To the Complaints Team,

I switched from my previous provider on 1 February and have been unable to resolve a simple billing query because your automated system is ineffective. I have now exhausted the normal channels. I run a small B&B in Northwich and my mobile signal is the lifeline that takes bookings and emergency calls. I need a clear plan: what is happening with the local mast, who is assigned to this, and when will coverage be reliably restored. I cannot accept another vague update. I am writing again regarding the final invoice issued after I ended my contract on 15 February 2026. I originally raised this on 22 February (case number 2026-0222) and received no substantive resolution. For context: I have a mobility impairment and rely on predictable billing and clear communication so I can manage my finances and medical appointments. On 15 February I followed your published cancellation steps and requested a final bill by email. Instead I was charged a further £48.99 on 18 February, which appeared as an unexpected debit to my account and left me short for essential prescriptions. I spoke with Sophie on 22 February who noted the charge but said she could not correct it without a supervisor review; I was told that someone would call me back within five days, but no call came. I also emailed billing@company on 25 February with copies of the cancellation confirmation and bank statement; no substantive reply was received. Please provide a detailed explanation of why the charge occurred, a full refund of £48.99 to the debit card used on 18 February, and a corrected final invoice. Given my health condition, I ask that you prioritise this matter and confirm by return with a named contact who will handle the refund. I would prefer an email so I have a written record, and I would appreciate clear step-by-step information about the timescale for resolution.

I will keep this precise because I've gone through the script already and I'm done repeating the same steps. Expected speed: 200 Mbps (fibre). Actual: 9–14 Mbps during weekday evenings. Speed tests run on 20 March (11:12pm) and 22 March (9:03pm) from my laptop (Windows 11, Ethernet directly into the Hub 5) returned 11.2 Mbps and 9.4 Mbps respectively. Uploads are fine, latency spikes to 85–120 ms at the worst times. I rebooted the router, replaced the ethernet cable, created a wired connection, and ran three separate tests with the provider's own diagnostics tool. I logged a fault on 23 March, spoke to Daniel in tech support who opened case BB-98765 and arranged an engineer visit for 24 March. The engineer, Samuel, replaced the wall socket and said 'it should be better' but speeds were unchanged.

I am not looking for immediate punitive action or refunds — I can cope for a few days — but this is impacting me during work hours when I need consistent upload/download performance for VPNs and cloud builds. I've escalated through normal channels already and have had no meaningful technical explanation beyond 'congestion'. Show me the capacity and contention figures for my exchange, show me the historical per‑hour bandwidth available to my cabinet, and explain why a supposedly 200 Mbps product falls to under 10 Mbps at predictable peak times. If there's a plan mismatch or a line profile error, fix it. If the issue is genuine network congestion, set out a timetable for upgrades or a reasonable mitigation. I'm asking for a manager review and, failing that, instructions for escalation to the ombudsman. I expect a technical report and a named contact within five working days.

I would also like written confirmation of the steps you will take to ensure no other family on a shared plan faces this lapse. If you cannot resolve this in the next business day I will escalate to Ofcom and seek alternative arrangements to safeguard my mother and children.

The sequence is as follows: 5 Mar charged £142.50, 10 Mar call with Megan who promised reversal, 17 Mar follow-up call with escalation promise, 24 Mar attempted online chat which closed with no resolution. I rely on that money for prescription payments and a care support top-up; the unexpected deduction has put me at risk of missing essential medication. I require an immediate reversal of the £114.00 overpayment to my bank and written confirmation that my account will be returned to the correct monthly amount of £28.50. Please also provide a formal apology and an explanation of how you will prevent this from recurring. Family Home 350 plan: evening speeds have dropped to 8–14Mbps since 06/03/2026. Wired speedtests at 20:30 show 12Mbps. Rebooted hub and checked cables. Children cannot do homework with this. What are you going to do about restoring the advertised throughput?

Right. So, my shiny 'new' phone decides to play dead five days after my student discount kicks in. Perfect timing. I want either a free replacement handset or a full refund for the handset portion of my contract. I will not accept another runaround. Please confirm how you will resolve this and when I can expect a usable phone.

I bought my handset on a student plan less than eight months ago and it has developed persistent faults that make it unreliable for day-to-day use. I cannot afford an out-of-warranty repair fee (quoted at around £75 when I asked), and repeated troubleshooting has not fixed the device. I would be grateful for a prompt solution: either an immediate replacement handset under the contract warranty or a prepaid collection for diagnostics and free repair. Please confirm next steps and a firm timescale within three working days so I can plan around my studies; I am disappointed this has taken several contacts without a durable outcome.

I've been with your company for 12 years and this is the first time I've felt I needed to ask for formal escalation. I am disappointed that after a decade with you I have had to reach this point, but I want a clear timeline for resolution within the next five working days.

On Monday 16 March my handset started shutting down mid-call while I was on a delivery run. I run a small courier operation and that phone is my primary way to accept bookings, navigate, and confirm drops. By 18 March I had spoken to your agent Aisha, who logged fault reference 90217 and said a replacement would be sent; I have received nothing and the device now fails to hold a charge for more than 30 minutes. I raised the fault once already and was promised a loan handset; that did not happen. I need either an immediate replacement handset or a working loan device while mine is assessed, and I need a record of the lost income covered or reimbursed. Please advise how you will get a working device to me within the next two working days so I can resume operations.

My name is Margaret L. Kemp and I am writing because I have been sold a contract I never agreed to. On 20 February 2026 I took a telephone call from one of your sales agents — he identified himself as Paul — who said he was helping me move my existing line. I explicitly stated that I am not confident with technology and requested the simplest option: keep my current pay-as-you-go landline only, with no new equipment and no fixed-term plan. Mr Paul proceeded to enter details and, without explanation or my meaningful consent, signed me up to a 24-month fibre package with an upfront router charge and a monthly direct debit of £36.00. I did not ask for that product. I do not want that product. I cancelled the direct debit on 28 February 2026 when I discovered the first bill and called your helpline the same day. I spoke to Maya in billing who said she would “note my account” and that someone would call back. No callback was made. I have since phoned on 3 March and 11 March (spoke to Jordan and then to a retention agent) and exhausted the usual routes; each time I had to repeat that I was mis-sold the contract and that I am not able to manage complicated equipment or online set-up. Your agents have not corrected the matter. I am aware of my rights under the Consumer Contracts Regulations regarding information and cancellation, and under the Consumer Rights Act where services must be performed as described. I require immediate rescission of the contract entered on my behalf on 20 February 2026, a full refund of any charges taken since then, and confirmation in writing that no early-termination fee or other liability will be applied. If this is not resolved within 14 days I will forward a formal complaint to your complaints manager and refer the matter to the Ombudsman for Telecommunications. I am an elderly person and this has caused financial and personal distress; I do not have the patience for further delay. Please escalate to a manager and confirm action by return.

I switched my home and phone service to your company from Sky under order reference 778811 on 14/02/2026. The installation appointment was booked for 17/02/2026 and the porting of my number was due to complete on that day. Since then there has been no substantive communication: the last update on the portal was 17/02/2026 and I have had two calls to your helpdesk (Kate on 23/02, case 778811-23K) and a complaints email reply on 03/03/2026 that offered no timeline. I have now exhausted the normal channels and require escalation to a manager or the Ombudsman if you cannot supply a firm plan immediately. Under the standards expected of new customer migrations, you are obliged to keep me informed of progress and any delays; your silence has left me unable to confirm the switch with my contacts, and I have lost an evening of work waiting for the engineer who did not arrive. Please provide within 48 hours: (1) a confirmed date and two-hour slot for installation and number porting, (2) a precise explanation for the delay since 17/02/2026 and why I received no interim updates, and (3) details of compensation you will offer for the time and inconvenience caused. If you fail to respond with a satisfactory plan, I will take this further via alternative dispute resolution channels.

I run a small catering business and purchased ten company handsets and a business line from you on 12 January 2026 (account 44-19-221). Since 05 March 2026 multiple handsets show repeated SIM errors and no network registration. I have called support on 06 March and 11 March, spoken to Mark and then to Lydia, and completed every diagnostic your scripted agents suggested. The faults have rendered three of my delivery drivers unable to take orders or update customers. I have documented loss of earnings totalling £1,240. I have now exhausted the normal complaint routes and the issue remains. Under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 the goods supplied must be fit for purpose and of satisfactory quality. These handsets are clearly not. I am therefore formally requesting immediate remedial action: either full replacement of faulty devices within five working days or a pro rata refund for devices that fail to network, plus compensation for demonstrable loss. If you do not escalate this to a senior manager and provide a binding timetable by close of business on 29 March 2026 I will refer the matter to the Ombudsman and consider legal remedies to recover my losses. This is not a negotiation; it is a statement of my rights and the impact on my livelihood.

I am writing on behalf of my family plan account (Family Share 30GB, account holder: Rebecca Hardy) to complain about the absence of meaningful updates. I was promised an engineer visit and a working line three separate times: 12 March (appointment missed), 19 March (no-show, no call), and again 24 March when Liam spoke to me and said he would escalate and that Suki would call me with a revised slot and compensation.
